Design document master model
The document master is the highest class in the complex hierarchy (see also Complex document object). It contains the revision independent information for the document, which is made up from a number of common interfaces as
described below.
In the core SmartPlant Foundation schema, there are two pre-configured revisable document classes: a template document class that is used for storing templates for reports and a general design document class.
The interfaces are arranged in a comprehensive hierarchy for the document master class definitions of these two documents. The design document master realizes the ISPFDesignDocMaster interface and a template document master
realizes the ISPFTemplateMaster. These are their primary interfaces. All other interfaces implied by these two can also be realized by any document master class definition. Most of the interfaces realized are required so that they are
automatically instantiated and attached to the document master when it is created.
ISPFDesignDocumentMaster interface
This is the primary interface of the design master class.
Functionality configured on ISPFDesignDocumentMaster
The commands Cancel and Undo Cancel are configured on this interface.
ISPFTemplateMaster interface
This is the primary interface of the template document master class.
ISPFNonPublishedDocMaster interface
This interface is realized by the document master classes and is instantiated when the document is created interactively. When using SmartPlant integration, a separate interface is realized on its document classes and instantiated during
the publish process. This distinguishes the published and unpublished documents.
Functionality configured on ISPFNonPublishedDocMaster
The commands Delete, Edit Relationships, Terminate, and Update are configured on this interface.
ISPFDocumentMaster interface
This interface carries the relationship to the document revision and identifies the class as a document master of a revisable document. It can optionally be realized by the class to allow the document master to be created without a revision
or to reserve the document in a document index. See the configuration section for such an example in the vendor document class.
Functionality configured on ISPFDocumentMaster
The command Relationship Details is configured on this interface.
SPFDocumentRevisions relationship definition
This relationship links the master document to its revisions.
ISPFDocument interface
This interface carries the basic document properties for categorizing the document. These properties can be populated automatically based on a classification system, as described in Design document classification model. This interface is
required on all document master classes.
The SPFDocState property manages the life cycle state of the master, initially RESERVED and then ISSUED when the first revision is signed off.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFDocCategory
The document category*
New Design Document form*
SPFDocType
The document type*
New Design Document form*
SPFDocSubType
The document sub type*
New Design Document form*
SPFDocTitle
The document title
New Design Document form
SPFDocState
The document master state
Set internally in code
* The document category, type, and sub type properties are hidden from the user on the form and are set automatically by the classification tree. For more information, see Classified Object Configuration.
Functionality configured on ISPFDocument
The command View Related items is configured on this interface.
ISPFDocumentRevision interface
This interface carries the revision information and identifies the class as a document revision. This interface is described in more detail in the next section.
ISPFDocumentObject interface
This is a generic interface that must be realized by the document master, revision, and version classes. It is used to define abstract relationships to objects that can be related to more than one component of the complex document. An
example of this is the label and watermark relationship in Watermark and label model.
ISPFClassifiedItem interface
This interface links a document to the classification tree. For more information, see Classified Object Model.
ISPFSubscribableItem interface
This interface implements the subscription functionality. Most documents are configured so that the document master, revision, and version must realize this interface, ensuring that the user can subscribe to the actions on all three
components of the complex document.
Design document revision model
The core SmartPlant Foundation schema contains two pre-configured revisable document classes.
The design document revision realizes the ISPFDesignDocRevision interface and the template document revision realizes the ISPFTemplateRevision; these are their primary interfaces. All other interfaces implied by these two can also be
realized by any document revision class definition.
ISPFDesignDocRevision and ISPFTemplateRevision interfaces
These are the primary interfaces of the design and template document revision classes.
ISPFNonPublishedDocRevision interface
This interface is realized by the document revision classes and is instantiated when the document is created interactively. When using SmartPlant integration, a separate interface is realized on its document revision classes and
instantiated during the publish process. This distinguishes the published and unpublished documents.
Functionality configured on ISPFNonPublishedDocRevision
The commands Delete, Edit Relationships, Terminate, Update, Relationship Details, Sign Off, and Sign Off with Form are configured on this interface.
Details
Sign off - Sets the document to the current released revision, makes it official, and supersedes any previously released revisions. Document revisions that have been signed off are locked and cannot be checked out.
Sign Off with Form - Performs the same operations as a sign off, but also allows the user to provide comments, a label, and a watermark for the document.
ISPFDocumentRevision interface
This interface carries the relationship to the document master and version and identifies the class as a document revision.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFMajorRevision
Major revision, the first part of revision scheme, which normally changes when a document undergoes a major
change
Create/Revise form
SPFMinorRevision
Minor revision, the optional second part of revision scheme, which normally changes when a document
undergoes an intermediate change and release
Create/Revise form
SPFRevState
The State property manages the life cycle state of the master, initially RESERVED
Set internally in code
SPFRevIssueDate
Date the revision is signed off
Set internally in code
SPFRevUnderChangeInSameConfig
The document can be revised into any number of configurations but only one at a time in the same
configuration
Set internally in code
SPFExternalRevision
External revision is a concatenation of major and minor revision
Set internally in code.
SPFApprovalDate
Date that can be set prior to the sign off of a revision - typically set in workflows
Can control method access through conditions.
Update workflows
SPFSignoffComments
Sign off comments
Sign off dialog
ISPFDocRevisionScheme interface
Document revisions can use different revision schemes throughout the life cycle. For example, a different revision scheme might be used for the as-built documents of the plant than those defining the changes in the project. Further details
can be found in the section Configure different revision scheme strategies.
SPFDocRevisionRevisionScheme relationship definition
See the SPFDocRevisionRevisionScheme relationship definition description in Revision scheme configuration.
SPFDocRevisionVersions relationship definition
Links the document revision to the document versions.
SPFDesignBasis relationship definition
This relates one revision to the next to allow navigation through the document history. This specifically identifies the basis of one revision to the next, and it is not necessarily sequential through the revisions. Old revisions can be revised
over the latest ones, and historical revisions with no design basis can be inserted in the sequence.
ISPFDocumentVersion interface
This interface carries the version information and identifies the class as a document version. For more information, see the ISPFDocumentVersion interface description in Design document version model.
ISPFDocumentObject
See the ISPFDocumentObject interface description in Design document revision model.
ISPFPlantItem interface
This is a generic interface that can be realized on items such as tags and assets. Objects that instantiate this interface can be related to the document revision. Equipment published by the integrated authoring tools have their own
relationships to the document revision.
SPFDocRevisionPlantItem relationship definition
This relationship links objects instantiated by the ISPFPlantItem interface to the document revision.
ISPFMergableDocumentRevision interface
For more information, see ISPFMergableDocumentRevision interface under Configuration controlled documents model.
ISPFMergableItem interface
For more information, see ISPFMergableItem interface under Configuration controlled documents model.
ISPFMergedItem interface
The ISPFMergableDocumentRevision interface, ISPFMergableItem interface, and ISPFMergedItem interface must be realized by a document revision in order to be configuration controlled and merged from a project to a plant. See
Configuration controlled documents model for more details.
ISPFSubscribableItem
For more information, see ISPFSubscribableItem interface in the section Design document revision model.
ISPFWorkflowItem interface
When a document is submitted to a workflow, it is normal for the revision to pass to other workflows. To enable this, the ISPFWorkflowItem interface is realized by the document revision classes. A typical workflow includes the review,
approval, and sign off of a document revision. If it is necessary for a document master to be passed through a workflow, for example to manage the creation of new revisions, then this interface could be realized by the document master
class.
See Workflow Model.
Revision scheme configuration
The system needs to be able to configure a revision scheme based on the classification of the document, the configure level at which the document exists, or the configuration to which the document belongs.
Interactive retrieval of revision schemes for a document takes the following preference:
Get all related revisions schemes for the interface of the document through the SPFInterfaceDefRevisionScheme relationship. If none are returned, get all revision schemes that do not have an SPFInterfaceDefRevisionScheme
relationship.
Then take:
First those that match configuration and classification of the document
Then those that match the classification and are set up for the same configuration level
Then those that match the classification and are set up for all configurations
Then those that match the configuration and are set up for all classifications
Then those that are set up for this configuration level and any classification
SmartPlant integration internally retrieves document revisions in a slightly different way, where the revision scheme is retrieved for the class and type of the document as the primary criteria.
ISPFDocumentRevision interface
See the Design document revision model.
SPFDocRevisionRevisionScheme relationship definition
Relates the appropriate revision scheme to a document revision.
ISPFRevisionScheme interface
This is the primary interface of the SPFRevisionScheme class.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFTakeNextMajorOnMerge
If the revision scheme is being used at the plant has this property set, the revision is merged as the next
available major revision at the plant when a revision is merged up from a project to a plant, for example.
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFConfigLevel
The configuration level to which the revision scheme is applicable
1 = Plant
2 = Project
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMinorSequenceType
The minor revision sequence type (sequence, repeating sequence, or numeric)
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFCustomRevision
A custom revision scheme. (Not validated by SmartPlant Foundation.)
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMajorSequence
The major sequence value
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMajorSequenceType
The major revision sequence type (sequence, repeating sequence, or numeric)
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMajorSequenceMinLength
Major sequence minimum length (only applicable if Major Sequence Type numeric is selected)
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMajorSequencePadChar
The pad character for the major sequence revision code (only applicable if a numeric Major Revision Type is
selected)
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMinorSequence
The minor sequence value
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMinorSequenceMinLength
Minor sequence minimum length (only applicable if Minor Sequence Type numeric is selected)
New/Update Revision Scheme form
SPFMinorSequencePadChar
The pad character for the minor sequence revision code (only applicable if a numeric Major Revision Type is
selected)
New/Update Revision Scheme form
The revision number used for the latest current document revision, not the superseded revision being revised, determines the next minor revision number. The scope for retrieving the “latest” revision depends on the unique key of the
revision. If it contains the configuration, then the scope is just the current configuration, but if it contains level, then it is all plants or all projects. If it contains neither, it looks across the whole system.
The major and minor sequences together define the revision scheme. For example, A for the major sequence and 1 for the minor sequence would create a revision of A1.
Functionality configured on ISPFRevisionScheme
The commands Edit Relationships, Copy, Terminate, and Update Relationship Details are configured on this interface for managing the revision schema definitions.
SPFInterfaceDefRevisionScheme relationship definition
Revision schemes are configured for different document classes by using this relationship. If the revision scheme is related to a general interface, such as ISPFDocumentRevision, then it would be appropriate for all document classes. To
make it more specific, the revision scheme should be related to a more specialized interface, maybe even the primary interface of the class.
ISPFObjClass interface
This interface identifies an object as a member of a classification tree, for example, 3D documents.
SPFRevisionSchemeObjClass relationship definition
This relationship is used to link the revision scheme to the classification tree object (document type).
ISPFConfiguration item interface
This interface identifies an object as a configuration item, such as a plant of a project.
SPFRevisionSchemeConfigurationItem relationship definition
This relationship is used to link the revision scheme to the configuration.
Examples of how to configure different revision schemes are found in the section Revision schemes configuration.
Design document version model
The document version is the lowest class in the complex hierarchy as described previously. It contains the version-specific information for the document and the relationship to the attached files.
The core SmartPlant Foundation schema contains two pre-configured revisable document classes.
The design document version realizes the ISPFDesignDocVersion interface, and the template document version realizes the ISPFTemplateVersion; these are their primary interfaces. All other interfaces implied by these two can also be
realized by any document version class definition.
ISPFDesignDocVersion and ISPFTemplateVersion interface
These are the primary interfaces of the design and template document version classes.
ISPFNonPublishedDocVersion interface
This interface is realized by the document version classes and is instantiated when the document is created interactively. When using SmartPlant integration, a separate interface is realized on its document version classes and instantiated
during the publish process. This distinguishes the published and unpublished documents.
Functionality configured on ISPFNonPublishedDocVersion
The commands Change Owning Group, Delete, Terminate, Run Editor, Undo Check Out, Update Reference Relationships, Update Referred by Relationships, Check In, Check In Replace, and Check Out are configured on this
interface.
Details
Change Owning Group - Allows you to change the owning group with which the document is associated, effectively changing which users have access to check out the document, based on user role assignments.
Run Editor - Allows you to open one or more of the attached files in the default application associated with the file extensions of the files.
Undo Check Out - Reverses the checkout of documents, sets them back to their original state, and removes the local files from the directory, where appropriate.
Update Reference Relationships - Updates any reference file relationships, a link to old versions of the document.
Update Referred by Relationships - Updates any referred by file relationships, a link to old versions of the document.
Check In - Checks selected documents into the vault, transferring the local files and, optionally, overwriting the previous document version.
Check In Replace - Checks selected documents into the vault, transferring the local files and overwriting the previous document version.
Check Out - Checks out selected documents to create new versions of the same revisions and copy the files to the user's computer.
ISPFDocumentVersion interface
This interface carries the relationship to the document revision and identifies the class as a document version.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFDocVersion
The document version number
Set internally in code
SPFDocVersionCheckedOut
Reserves the version for the exclusive edit of a user and the related file to the local computer for editing
Set internally in code
SPFDocVersionSuperseded
Version has been superseded
Set internally in code
SPFDocVersionSupersededDate
Date the version was superseded
(Set to the creation date of the next version.)
Set internally in code
Functionality configured on ISPFDocumentVersion
The commands New External Transmittal, New Internal Transmittal, Show Nested References, External Transmittal History, and Internal Transmittal History are configured on this interface.
ISPFDocumentObject interface
See the Design document master model.
ISPFSubscribableItem interface
See the Design document master model.
ISPFFTRItem interface
The Full Text Retrieval (FTR) engine available in SmartPlant Foundation is controlled by this interface. Document versions and objects that have this interface support the commands to submit the object metadata and associated files to
the scheduler for indexing.
ISPFOwnedObj interface
This interface must be realized by every document version. It is used to relate a checkout user to the document version. The relationship to owning group from this interface is not used at the version level of the document interface.
ISPFFileComposition interface
The files attached to a document are related to the version through this interface.
SmartPlant Markup Plus can be used to create, comment, and markup layers on top of any file, but most typically drawings. Commands are available on the document version, file, and markup layer for locking these markup layers. The
Markups Locked property, when set on the document version, prevents markups on the files associated to that version.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFMarkupsLocked
Prevents markups on the files associated to the version
Shortcut menu command
SPFArchiveDate*
Date on which files were archived
Shortcut menu command
* Archive date - files can be archived to an alternative store external to SmartPlant Foundation; this property records the date on which that was done. The files are not removed from the system during the archive process. See Configure
the archive and purge functionality for more details.
Functionality configured on ISPFFileComposition
The commands Archive Files, Convert for Navigation, Save Target As, Print, Generate PDF Copies, Generate WebGL Viewer Files, Lock Markups, View in SmartPlant Preview, Unlock Markups, and View and Markup are
configured on this interface.
Document reservation configuration
One of the roles of the document controller is to manage the document index. In many projects, it is necessary to reserve the document numbers before creating the document files. When the document is reserved on the index, the
revision details may not be known. To support this, SmartPlant Foundation provides a mechanism for creating document masters without their revisions and versions. This is important, as in many situations, more data is needed when the
document is finally created. This can be modeled on the document revision as required properties and relationships so that they have to be entered when the reserved document is activated. The document can still be created as a
complete document master, revision, and version.
Document reservation model
To enable a document master to be created without a document revision, the ISPFDocMaster interface is realized optionally on the class definition. If this was realized required, then every document master would be created with the
ISPFDocMaster interface, and because this has a required relationship to the revision, it would force the creation of a revision and version as well.
Document reservation is the creation of a document master without optional interfaces. Additional interfaces are added to the class definition to perform the document reservation and activation, as described below.
Document reservation support is provided as follows.
ISPFDocMaster interface
This interface is realized optionally.
ISPFReservableDocumentItem interface
This interface is realized and required.
ISPFReservableItem interface
This interface is realized and required and caries the activate functionality.
Document reservation and forms
To reserve a document, it is necessary to create a form for the document master and relate it to the document master class.
The form for reserving the document master has the following standard sections:
Name
Display as
Purpose
Level
DocNameDesc_C-Section
Main details
Activate, Revise, Copy, Create
$1/$2/$3
ISPFReservedDocument-Section**
Document properties
Copy, Create, Query, Update, GetInfo
Not specified as it is used on a vendor doc
master form on a single level
DocGenDetails_QI-Section
General details
GetInfo, Query
$3
** This section is different from the regular document form section in that it has a different display item for the document state that has a default value of RESERVED_INACTIVE.
A form can still be created for a document version for the regular creation of a complete document, as described in Basic document configuration. Because the ISPFDocumentMaster interface is optionally realized, the following hidden
section has to be created and attached to the form to ensure that this interface is instantiated.
Name
Display as
Purpose
ISPFDocumentMaster-Section
Document Master Interface
Create
To reserve a document, we do not instantiate ISPFDocumentMaster.
To create a document, we need to instantiate ISPFDocumentMaster, but this is an optional interface; it does not have any properties, so it is not created by default. The only way to force its creation is to create an interface display
item on the form.
Display item
Name: Doc-ISPFDocumentMasterIDef
Type: Interface Definition
Interface: ISPFDocumentMaster
Default Create value = True
This display item would appear on the form as a Boolean, but the whole section is hidden, as no user input is required.
Expose the document reservation in the GUI
To reserve a document, the create method is configured on the document master and displays the document master form without the revision and version form sections.
The document is created in the RESERVED_INACTIVE state from the display item on the ISPFReservedDocument-Section form section.
To reserve a basic document:
BasicDocumentReservation
On a drop-down menu
Client API
CreateClassObj
Class Definition of object to be created
BasicDocMASTERClassDef
Title
Basic Documents
The class definition to create is the document master.
Classified documents can be reserved in the same way, using the classified object APIs.
The Activate command is available on the shortcut menu of reserved documents from the ISPFReservableItem interface.
Snapshot document model
This section describes the snapshot model and how it is used by publishable documents and list documents. The snapshot creation is different for each type of document, which requires different snapshot interfaces on the document
revision classes that imply the ISPFDocumentRevision interface.
ISPFDatasheetRevSnapshot interface
This interface controls the snapshot process for data sheets. The base snapshot functionality is defined on the ISPFDocumentRevision interface, and there are overrides on ISPFDatasheetRevSnapshot for the data sheet snapshot
generation.
ISPFListDocRevSnapshot interface
This interface controls the snapshot process for list documents. The base snapshot functionality is defined on the ISPFDocumentRevision interface, and there are overrides on ISPFListDocRevSnapshot for the list document snapshot
generation.
List document model
The Snapshot document model shows the model for the list document revision class. As well as the ISPFListDocRevSnapshot interface, it has the ISPFListEditControl interface to manage editing of the list from the document.
ISPFListEditControl interface
To edit the list of items attached to a document or other object, the document revision object must realize the interface ISPFListEditControl. This is a required realized interface for the document revision object.
This interface exposes the following properties:
SPFPathDefnToRelatedItems - The path definition used to navigate to the list of related items.
SPFColSetToDisplayRelatedItems - The column set to use in the List Edit control for displaying the list of related items.
SPFTitleForDisplayingRelatedItems - The title to display in the List Edit control.
SPFClassDefForNewRelatedItems - The class definition to use to create new items in the List Edit control.
SPFDeleteActionForRelatedItems - The delete action to use in the List Edit control.
This interface is added onto the revision object when you create a document from the List Edit control.
Alternatively, you can use one of the three following client APIs to create the document:
CreateClassObjWithPropertyOverrides
CreateClassObjFromClassWithPropertyOverrides
CreateClassObjAndRelWithPropertyOverrides
For more information on argument 10 (property overrides), see Data sheet documents configuration.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
How it is set
SPFOriginalMethodUID
Identifies the method that launches the List Edit control for the list.
Set when the document is created from the List Edit control.
SPFOriginalPathDefn
Path definition (prefixed +/-) of the relationship definition to use when relating list items to
new or existing item.
Set when the document is created.
Publishable documents model
The Snapshot document model shows the model for the data sheet document revision class. This is an example of a publishable document in which the revision and version both realize additional interfaces to identify this behavior.
ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentRevision
For a document to be publishable, it must have a required realizes relationship between the class definition of the revision and the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentRevision interface definition. With this interface on the class, revisions are
automatically reserved in the data warehouse.
The SPFAuthPublishableTargetClassDef property on this interface defines the authoring tool that this document belongs to. This information is used to identify the correct registration details for the tool and the warehouse to reserve
revisions in. This value is populated automatically by the create method.
A typical value for an IPD data sheet document used for data sheet snapshots is provided below:
ProcessDocument. For example, the CreateDocFromTag and CreateSPFLLALineListRecordDocument methods have argument 10 set to stamp the value.
ProcessDocument onto the SPFAuthPublishableTargetClassDef property. This ensures that the property SPFTEFPublishedDocClassDefUID on ISPFTEFPublishedDocMaster is updated.
This value is different for other types of documents that reserve revisions.
 In the default authoring database, the line list document and data sheet document class definitions realize the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentRevision and ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion interface definitions. This means
that you cannot create one of these documents without SmartPlant Foundation being registered to a SmartPlant Foundation data warehouse. If you want to create these documents in a stand-alone authoring system, these interfaces need
to be removed from the class definitions for line lists and data sheets. To remove the interfaces, load SCHEMA_RemovePublishableDocRealizeRels.xml available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For
more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion
To compare and publish a document, it must have a required realizes relationship between the class definition of the version and the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion interface definition. With this interface on the class, the compare
and publish snapshot methods are available if the conditions on the methods are met.
Data sheet document model
The Snapshot document model shows the model for the data sheet document revision class. This is both publishable and supports snapshot generation prior to publish. Both the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentRevision and the
ISPFDatasheetRevSnapshot interfaces are required on the data sheet document revision class to be able to create and publish the document.
ISPFDatasheetDocRevision
This is the only interface on the data sheet document class definitions that has specific functionality that allows the document to be related to a tag. It also stores the name of the relationship to the tag in the property
SPFRelToDatasheetObj. This allows the snapshot functionality to navigate from the document to the tag to create view and XML files of the data sheet, which are then stored in the vault and related to the latest version for this revision.
SPFDatasheetDocType is used to match the SPFDatasheetType property on data sheet definitions to determine which data sheet definition is relevant to the document when creating a snapshot. An example value is IPD. These values are
populated from the create method for the data sheet document.
Configuration controlled documents
Documents, like any object, can be created as configuration-independent (visible in any plant or project) or as configuration-controlled. When they are configuration-controlled, they can be revised into lower configurations (from plant to
project) and merged back again.
Configuration controlled documents model
The class definitions for the master, revision, and version need to be configured with the following properties:
Configuration Controlled = True.
The document master key can be variously configured.
Unique key definition = Prefix,ConfigLevel1,Name to allow documents with the same name to exist in different plants.
 You can also use the unique key definition ConfigLevel1OrBlank to allow documents with the same name to exist in different plants as well as in Configuration Top. For example, if the unique key definition is set to
DM,ConfigLevel1OrBlank,name on the SPFDesignDocMaster class definition, then for a design document named 001:
the unique key will be DM_PlantA_001, if the user is creating the document in Plant A
the unique key will be DM_001, if the user is creating the document in Configuration Top
If the document revisions are to be merged back to the higher configurations, then additional interfaces are required on the document revision class.
ISPFMergableDocumentRevision interface
This interface is realized optionally. It is instantiated when the revision is revised into a lower configuration.
ISPFMergableItem interface
This is an optional realized interface and is instantiated when the revision is revised into a lower configuration and provides the merge and compare methods, as well as exposing the status properties. When an object or revision is merged
into a higher configuration, it is still maintained at the lower configuration to maintain history.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFMergedToHigherConfig
Has been merged to a higher configuration
Set internally in code
SPFMergeStatus
Merge status
Set internally in code
SPFDesignBasisResolutionRequired
The differences between this object and its original in the higher configuration have been resolved
Set internally in code
SPFPreventMergeInd
When the SPFPreventMergeInd property is set to True, the item cannot be merged. If set to False or not set,
then the item can be merged.
Set the property using custom code, or create display item, or
column item, and update it using forms/List edit
Functionality configured on ISPFMergableItem
The commands Resolve Conflicts, Compare Object to Higher Configuration, and Merge are configured on this interface.
Merge Method condition
By default, the SPFPreventMergeInd property does not exist on the Merge Method condition. When the SPFPreventMergeInd property is added to the Merge method and the SPFPreventMergeInd property is set to True, Merge is not
available using the shortcut menu.
ConflictsResolved condition
The path for the SPFConditionDefn in the ConflictsResolved condition must be updated to Obj.SPFDesignBasisResolutionRequired=False AND Obj.SPFPreventMergeInd=False.
<SPFCondition>
    <IObject UID="CND_ConflictsResolved" Name="ConflictsResolved" Description="Conflicts have been resolved" ContainerID="Core.ConfigAdmin_Admin" />
    <ISPFCondition SPFConditionDefn="Obj.SPFDesignBasisResolutionRequired=False AND Obj.SPFPreventMergeInd=False" />
   <ISPFAdminItem />
    <ISPFSubscribableItem />
  </SPFCondition>
ISPFMergedItem interface
This optional realized interface is instantiated on merge and exposes the merge history information.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFMergedFromConfig
The configuration from which it was merged
Set internally in code
SPFMergeDate
The date when it was merged
Set internally in code
Mergable documents and forms
There are no special considerations for these. A section to display the merge status could be added to the form, but it is more usual to see these through the column set.
Expose document merge GUI
As described above, this is provided through the realized interfaces.
See Classified Object Model for more details.
Data sheet documents configuration
Data sheet documents are used to store a snapshot of the data sheet that will ultimately be publishable to the warehouse. Previously, the only way to publish a data sheet was to create a data sheet and publish it immediately. It is not
possible to circulate a data sheet for review and then publish it if it was approved. This is because if you look at a data sheet and decide it contains the data that you want to publish, you cannot determine if someone else altered the
underlying data since you opened and populated the data sheet.
The data sheet document snapshot provides a way for the engineer to take a snapshot of the data at a particular point in time and publish that data for other tools to use.
The data sheet documents provided in the authoring layer automatically reserve revisions in the warehouse. This means that the authoring tool and the warehouse maintains the same revision details when the document is published. The
reserving of revisions is controlled by the presence of the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentRevision interface. In the delivered model, the interface is a required realizes, so the functionality is available by default.
The workflow for using the data sheet documents is as follows:
Find the relevant tag, and right-click it. An option is available to Create Document for IPD from the IPRCProcessInstrument interface. This creates a new document and relates it to the tag.
The snapshot is then created from the shortcut menu for the document.
 An ENS definition determines the name of the data sheet document, based on the name of the related tag.
This create method is based on the CreateClassObjAndRelWithPropertyOverrides client API. This API is the same as CreateClassObjAndRel. See CreateClassObjAndRel in Classified Object Model for a description of the other
arguments.
The difference with this API is that argument 10 (property overrides) is used for setting properties on named interfaces.
In this case, we are defining the Tool ID property to be stamped on the ISPFAuthPublishable interface and the SPFDatasheetDocType property to be stamped on the ISPFDatasheetDocRevision interface.
The argument takes the form InterfaceDef.PropertyDef.Value; if there is more than one, they are separated by a tilde (~).
The API also takes argument 4 (the relationship definition to related object) and records it in a property on the data sheet revision so the snapshot command can derive how to get back to the IPD tag, along with the base functionality of
creating the relationship between the object that the method is on and the object being created.
The method also defines the classification of the document, which must match the classification that the TEFPublished document (the reserved revisions) will take on.
Data sheet documents compare configuration
Comparing snapshot revisions or versions is controlled by the presence of the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion interface definition. In the delivered model, the interface is a required realizes relationship so the functionality is
available by default.
The publishable document compare uses the existing DocVersionCompare method. This method was previously only available on published documents, but has now been made available to publishable documents (snapshot documents)
through the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion interface definition.
Data sheet documents publish configuration
The publishing of snapshot revisions is controlled by the presence of the ISPFAuthPublishableDocumentVersion interface definition. In the delivered model, the interface is a required realizes relationship, so the functionality is available by
default.
There is a new method for Publish Snapshot called FrameworkPublishAuthDoc. This method uses the existing FrameworkPublishRHM client API. There is a condition on this method that checks to see if there are snapshot files
attached to the data sheet document. If there are no files attached, then the method is not available. The method is only available if the snapshot files have been generated and are attached to the document.
Full Text Retrieval
Full Text Retrieval (FTR) allows you to index and search for files, attributes or document content (including text) in files. The actual locations of the text occurrences within the files are also identified. The files can be text files, word
processing files (such as Microsoft® Word and WordPerfect), and CAD files (such as MicroStation, SmartSketch®, and AutoCAD). However, only the text portions of the CAD files are indexed and available for searching.
All the FTR configuration settings are modeled in the SmartPlant schema. A new class definition called SPFFTROptions has been added to the core schema whose which contains all the FTR configuration information. This class definition
realizes three interfaces; ISPFFTRConfig, ISPFFTRServerConfig, and ISPFFTRStopProperty. All FTR configuration keys are exposed as property definitions by these interfaces.
ISPFFTRConfig interface
This interface exposes FTRCollectionName, FTRIndexingType, FTRANDToken, FTRORToken, FTRNOTToken, FTRLocaleList, FTRCharCode, FTRTermGen, FTRDocLocale, FTRAttrLocale, FTRRelevanceMethod, and
FTRRewindIndexLogFlag property definitions.
ISPFFTRServerConfig interface
This interface exposes FTRMainServerHost, FTRMainServerVirDir, and FTRSSDriverName property definitions. FTRSSDriverName contains the ODBC driver name for the Search Server.
ISPFFTRStopProperty interface
This interface exposes the FTRStopProperties property definition. This property contains all the stop properties, which do not require indexing, in a comma separated list.
Watermark and label model
Watermarks and labels may be applied to printed documents. Labels are pieces of text written to the header or footer of the document or drawing, and watermarks are pieces of text overlaid across the entire documents.
The watermark and label definitions are created as objects in SmartPlant Foundation and then related to the document to either the master or revision. The printing software then reads these objects and processes them. To assist with a
relationship, navigation and abstract relationships are defined and specialized at the document master and revision level.
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these interfaces are given in the sections that follow.
ISPFDocument interface
See the Design document master model.
ISPFDocumentMaster interface
See the Design document master model.
ISPFDocumentRevision interface
See the Design document master model.
ISPFDocumentObject interface
See the Design Document Master Model.
ISPFLabel interface
This interface holds the label properties, such as position of label, font style, and size.
The following properties are found on this interface.

SPFLabelTopLeft
Position label top left

SPFLabelTopCenter
Position label top center
Create/Update Label form

Position label top right
Create/Update Label form
SPFLabelBottomLeft
Position label bottom left
Create/Update Label form
SPFLabelBottomCenter
Position label bottom center
Create/Update Label form
SPFLabelBottomRight
Position label bottom right
Create/Update Label form
SPFLabelFontName
Label font name
Create/Update Label form
SPFLabelFontSize
Label font size
Create/Update Label form
SPFLabelFontStyle
Label font style
Create/Update Label form
Functionality configured on ISPFLabel
The commands Copy, Terminate, and Update are configured on this interface.
SPFDocMasterLabel relationship definition
This relationship definition is used to relate the label to the document master (can only relate to one).
SPFDocRevisionLabel relationship definition
This relationship definition is used to relate the label to the document revision (can only relate to one). This overrides the relationship set on the master.
SPFDocumentObjectLabel relationship definition
This is an abstract relationship for easy navigation.

This interface holds the watermark text position and style.
The following properties are found on this interface.

SPFWatermarkText

SPFWatermarkType
Type of watermark

SPFWatermarkPosition
Position of watermark
Create/Update Watermark form

Watermark font
Create/Update Watermark form
SPFWatermarkFontSize
Watermark font size

Watermark font style

Functionality configured on ISPFWatermark
The commands Copy, Terminate, and Update are configured on this interface.
SPFDocMasterWatermark relationship definition
This relationship definition is used to relate the watermark to the document master (can only relate to one).
SPFDocRevisionWatermark relationship definition
This relationship definition is used to relate the watermark to the document revision (can only relate to one). This overrides the one set on the master.
SPFDocumentObjectWatermark relationship definition
This is an abstract relationship for easy navigation.
File interface model
All files have a common behavior controlled by the ISPFFile and ISPFBusinessFile interfaces. All file classes realize these interfaces; this provides the relationship to the parent business object, such as the document version, and the file
interface stores the physical file location, its ownership, and its type.
Additional interfaces control more specific behavior for the files used in data sheets, data lists, and files that are persisted in the database rather than a vault.
Two file classes are delivered with a system: a template file that is persisted in the database and a general design file used to persist files in a vault. There are additional file classes for Markup files and alternate renditions, for example.
ISPFTemplateFile interface
This is the primary interface of the template file class.
ISPFDesignFile interface
This is the primary interface of the design file class.
ISPFBusinessFile interface
This interface manages the file as a business object. It indicates the file is attached to a business object through the SPFFileComposition relationship.
File classes for Markup, alternate rendition, and navigation files do not have this interface, as they are not related to a business object but related directly to other files. This is described in more detail in the next section.
The view and edit properties are by default based on the file type, which is in turn identified by the file name extension. The 3D model property is set to No by default, but is automatically set to Yes for Smart 3D (.zvf) and Smart Review
(.vue) file types.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFEditInd
File is editable
Create form (based on file type)
SPFViewInd
File is viewable
Create form (based on file type)
SPF3DModelInd
File is a 3D model
Create form
SPFFileCheckedOut
File is checked out
Check Out and Check In forms
Functionality configured on ISPFBusinessFile
The commands Print (Edit watermarks/labels), Delete, Terminate, Update, Compare Files, Convert for Navigation, Save Target As, View in SmartPlant Review, View Alternate, View Native, View and Markup, and Print (local)
are configured on this interface.
SPFFileComposition relationship definition
This relationship definition links the business file to the business objects, such as documents, and is the interface around which most file management functionality is written.
ISPFFile interface
This interface is realized by every file class and carries the information about the physical file.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFLocalDirectory
Local directory
Set using the Attach File form
SPFLocalFileName
Local file name
Set using the Attach File form
SPFRemoteFileName
Remote file name
Set internally in code
SPFLocalHostName
Local host name
Set internally in code
ISPFDBFile interface
This interface indicates that the file is persisted in the database rather than the vault. The template file class realizes this interface.
ISPFOwnedObj
In a similar manner to the document revision and version, a file is owned by an owning group when it is added to the system and by a user when it is checked out.
SPFFileFileType relationship definition
This relationship definition links the file to its file type. The file type is determined by matching file extension. The file type viewable and editable properties are used as defaults for the actual files properties on the ISPFBusinessFile
interface.
SPFFileVault relationship definition
This relationship definition links the file to its vault.
ISPFAuthToolDataFile interface
This interface is realized by the SPFDesignFile class definition and is used to identify the snapshot data file for compare and publish.
ISPFAuthToolViewFile interface
This interface is realized by the SPFDesignFile class definition and is used to identify the snapshot view file for compare and publish.
ISPFFileInfo interface
This interface is realized by the SPFDesignFile class definition and is implied by ISPFFile. ISPFFileInfo is used by the server during file upload to obtain the actual dimensions of a SmartPlant file format, which are used to aid in printing.
This operation is performed on the SmartPlant file types that have the File dimensions to be extracted option set.
The following are the properties on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFFileHeight
File height
During Attach and Check in
SPFFileWidth
File width
During Attach and Check in
SPFFileUnits
File units
During Attach and Check in
File composition model
When a file is registered in SmartPlant Foundation, it is stored in a vault or in the database as described in previous sections. These files are native to the application that created them and are not the best format for general distribution,
searching in SmartPlant Foundation, and general communication. For this reason, there are three tools available in SmartPlant Foundation, each of which has its own special file class:
Drawing annotation and Markup using SmartPlant Markup Plus
Alternate rendition generation (PDF)
FTR indexing
Each of these classes are related to the native file through a composition interface. The markup interface is always realized by the design file class as this carries commands that interact with SmartPlant Markup Plus. The other two are
added by the applications.
There is also a composition interface for relating reference files.
ISPFTemplateFile and ISPFDesignFile interfaces
These are the primary interfaces of the SPFTemplateFile and SPFDesignFile classes.
ISPFNavigationFileComposition interface
This interface is added by the navigation functionality.
It provides the functionality to generate, attach, and display the hotspot files. This interface is instantiated during the file create process if the related SPFFileType indicates that it requires the generation of the hot spot file.
SPFFileNavigationFile relationship definition
This is a specialization of the abstract SPFFileRelation and links the file to its hot spotted file.
ISPFNavigationFile interface
This is the primary interface of the hot spotted file.
ISPFAlternateRenditionComposition interface
This interface is added by the alternate rendition generation, see ISPFAlternateRendition interface and SPFFileAlternateRenditionRelationship definition for more details.
SPFFileAlternateRenditionRelationship definition
This relationship definition links the business file to its alternate rendition. It is used by the view commands to retrieve and view alternate renditions.
This is a specialization of the abstract SPFFileRelation.
ISPFAlternateRendition interface
This identifies the file as an alternate rendition and used by the issuing software.
ISPFMarkupComposition interface
This interface relates the markup layers to the files. Markup layers are created by SmartPlant Markup Plus through shortcut menu commands on this interface. The Markup layers are registered in SmartPlant Foundation and stored in the
same vault as the file.
The following property is found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFIsFileMarkupLocked
Set when markups are locked for that file
File shortcut menu command
Functionality configured on ISPFMarkupComposition
The commands Lock Markups and Unlock Markups are configured on this interface.
SPFFileMarkup relationship definition
This relationship definition links the markup files to the business file and is created and managed by the SmartPlant Foundation Markup application.
This is a specialization of the abstract SPFFileRelation.
ISPFMarkupFile interface
This is the primary interface of the markup file.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFMarkupColor
Color of markup layer
Markup options
SPFIsMarkupLocked
Set when markup is locked
Markup file shortcut menu command
SPFIsMarkupCheckedOut
Set when markup is being edited
Set internally in code using the markup application that is editing
the markup
Functionality configured on ISPFMarkupFile
The commands Change Owner and Owning Group, Lock Markups, Terminate, Unlock Markups, and Update are configured on this interface.
ISPFFile interface
See the File interface model.
SPFFileRelation relationship definition
This is an abstract relationship for easy navigation in code.
ISPFRefFile interface
Any file can reference another file, so all files have this interface.
ISPFRefFileComposition interface
This interface is added when SmartPlant Foundation detects that a file has reference files. This is done during file attach or check in. If the file has reference files, they are searched for in the system and related to the master file through
this interface.
Functionality configured on ISPFRefFileComposition
The commands Edit Reference Relationships, Show Nested Reference Files, and Relationship Details are configured on this interface.
SPFReferenceFile relationship definition
This relationship definition links reference files to the master files.
ISPFCopiedReferenceFile interface
This interface identifies a copied reference file.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on

Host where copied file is located
Set internally in code
SPFDirectory
Directory where copied file is located
Set internally in code
SPFCopiedFileName
The copied file name
Set internally in code
SPFReferenceCopy relationship definition
When a document is checked out, the system determines if any of its files have any reference files. If the reference files are not being checked out as well, they are copied to the client, and this relationship is created to keep track of them.
ISPFReferenceControl interface
This is the link interface on the SPFReferenceFile relationship definition and contains the SPFMaintainRels property. This controls how the reference relationships are managed during revise and sign off.
The following property is found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFMaintainRels
Controls how the reference relationships are managed during revise and sign off
Shortcut menu can edit relationships on the file that has
reference files
File types model
File types control the behavior of physical files stored in SmartPlant Foundation. The behavior control ranges from simple viewable and editable properties, to reference file processing, PDF generation control, FTR processing, printing, and
automatic title block population.
ISPFFileType interface
This interface contains the properties of the file types attached to a document.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFFileViewable
These files can be viewed by SmartPlant Markup Plus
New/Update form
SPFFileEditable
These files can be selected for checkout
New/Update form
SPFFileCheckedInWithMaster
If this is set to TRUE, files of this type are automatically checked in when their master files are checked in
New/Update form
SPFFileCheckedOutWithMaster
If this is set to TRUE, files of this type are automatically checked out when their master files are checked out
New/Update form
SPFFileExtension
Maps a file name to its file type (identifies the file type)
New/Update form
SPFFileFilterName
Defines the FTR generation process for that file type. There is a fixed set of possible values each linked to a
different process in the FTR engine.
New/Update form
SPFFileDefaultApplication
This is the default application associated with the file type. It is currently used to determine the printing
application if there is no printer relationship for the file.
New/Update form
SPFFileConvertForNavigationr
Identifies that this cannot be hot spotted directly but first needs to be converted into a Smart Sketch file (*.igr)
New update form
SPFDoNotcompressFile
If this is set to TRUE, files of this type are not compressed to view it. An example of a file type that does not
benefit from compression is a Smart 3D .zvf file.
 Hidden files (Viewable=FALSE and Editable=FALSE) are not listed in the client unless explicitly requested in method arguments.
Functionality configured on ISPFFileType
The commands Edit Relationships, Relationship Details, Copy, Terminate, and Update are configured on this interface.
SPFFileTypeFileType relationship definition
This relationship definition is used during the conversion of a file to a .pdf file or to any other alternate rendition, as well as during the generation of WebGL viewer files (.binz and .vecz). It has a linked interface,
ISPFAlternateRenditionInfo, that has properties to determine the application that will perform the conversion process. These properties are not required for generating WebGL viewer files, but you must have Intergraph Smart® Interop
Publisher 2017 R1 installed on your SmartPlant Foundation File Server. For more information, see File Service Settings node properties.
 The SPFFileTypeFileType relationship definition supports multi-staged conversions.
ISPFTitleBlockPluginAddress interface
This is a link interface and provides an update plug-in address for a specific file type and document type.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFPluginAddress
The COM program ID or .NET class name
(This needs to be specified with the ProgID if the plug-in is implemented as a COM component.)
Shortcut menu Edit relationships on file type
SPFPluginPath
The .NET DLL path (This needs to be specified if the plug-in is implemented as a .NET component.)
Create relationship and shortcut menu Edit relationships on file
type
ISPFAlternateRenditionInfo interface
This is a link interface that holds the properties to provide the application that is to be used for conversion.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFAlternateRendition
Application
This is a command (application name including the arguments) used to generate the output file. This needs to
be populated for file conversion between any two file types.
Create relationship and shortcut menu Edit relationships on file
type
SPFAlternateRenditionPath
This is used in case of multi-step process of alternate rendition.
In some cases, if there is no application to directly convert, then a multi-step process is used to get the
required conversion. The path information is provided on this property.
For example, the delivered model generates PDF files from Word files using a multi-step process. Initially,
Word is converted into PS, and then the PS is converted into PDF. In this case, the alternate rendition path is
as follows:
FT_WordFile/FT_PSFile/FT_PDFFile
Create relationship and shortcut menu Edit relationships on file
type
SPFFileFileType relationship definition
This relationship definition links the files to its file type.
SPFPrinterFileTypes relationship definition
This relationship definition links the file type to a specific printer. See How to Configure the Infrastructure Model for more details on printer configuration.
SPFFileTypeRefType relationship definition
This relationship definition is used to identify the reference file behavior managed by SmartPlant Markup Plus for the file type. Valid alternatives are ACAD, AOVL, OLE, and USTN.
 One file type may have more than one ref type. Similarly, one ref type may be used on several file types. Because of this, the maximum cardinality is * on this relationship for End1 and also for End2.
SPFFileTypeObjClass relationship definition
This relationship is used by the title block population functionality available through SmartPlant integration. This relationship is used to provide the title block plug-in details.
This relationship has a link interface, ISPFTitleBlockPluginAddress, which contains the information to determine the title block processing application for files of that file type and document classification.
ISPFFile interface
See the File interface model.
ISPFPrinter interface
See How to Configure the Infrastructure Model.
ISPFRefType interface
This is the primary interface of the SPFRefType class.
Vault configuration model
Below is a summary of the vaulting requirements:
Support the migration of SmartPlant Foundation 3.x systems. (In other words, support vaulting by owning group.)
Support different vaults based on configurations.
Support different vaults based on document classes and property values of the attaching object or a related object.
Support the creation of subdirectories under a vault based on property values of the attaching object or a related object.
Support the creation of subdirectories under a vault based on a maximum file count.
ISPFFile interface
See the File interface model.
ISPOwnedObj interface
See above and also Security Model.
ISPFVault interface
This is the primary interface of the SPFVault class.
See also Security Model.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFLocal Path
Where the files are actually placed on the server
New/Update Vault form
SPFSubDirectoryProperty
The name of the property of a file or related object to be used to name subdirectories when those files or
related objects are added to the vault
New/Update Vault form
SPFSubDirMaxFileCount
The maximum number of files to be stored in the vault
(0 = no limit)
New/Update Vault form
SPFSubDirCount
The number of files currently located in the directory specified in the local path
New/Update Vault form
Functionality configured on ISPFVault
The commands Edit Relationships, Copy, Terminate, Update, and Relationship Details are configured on this interface.
Notes on properties
The SPFSubDirectoryProperty property takes a string that defines a property on the file or related object that is to be used for creating sub directories under the vault. The format of this string is the same as that used for conditions.
Obj.PropertyName
Obj->RelDef_[12|21].PropertyName
Examples for subdirectories based on document type (SPFSubDirectoryProperty) are:
For a file attached to a document version:
Obj->SPFFileComposition_12->SPFRevisionVersions_21->SPFDocumentRevisions_21-> SPFClassifiedLevel2_12.Name.
 The above example is using multiple relationship navigation.
For a file attached directly to a document master (Admin document):
Obj->SPFFileComposition_12->SPFClassifiedLevel2_12.Name.
The SPFClassifiedLevel2_12 relationship gets the classification tree instead of using the SPFDocType property as this is not always populated (only when there is an enum hierarchy mapped to the classification tree).
The SPFSubDirMaxFileCount property is used to indicate a maximum number of files for a subdirectory. Subdirectories are created and named (1, 2, 3, and so forth) as the file limit is reached.
Examples of subdirectories by file count are:
SPFSubDirMaxFileCount = 10000
Results in:
.\VaultPath\1\ for the first 10000 files
.\VaultPath\2\ for files 10001 to 20000
SPFSubDirCount records the number of subdirectories currently created to prevent repeated searching of the file system for the latest one during file transfer.
ISPFVaultRule interface
This is a link interface to link the vault rules to a condition.
ISPFDataAccessGroup interface
See Security Model.
Design document classification model
See Classified Object Model.
Template document classification model
See Classified Object Model.
Alternate rendition model
ISPFAlternateRendition interface
This is the primary interface of the SPFAlternateRendition class.
SPFFileAlternateRendition relationship definition
This relationship definition links the file to the alternate rendition.
ISPFAlternateRenditionComposition interface
This interface holds the alternative viewable file attachments.
Functionality configured on ISPFAlternateRenditionComposition
The commands Save Alternate Rendition, Generate PDF Copy, Generate View Rendition, and Generate Web File are configured on this interface.
ISPFDesignFile interface
This interface optionally realizes ISPFAlternateRenditionComposition, which provides the functionality to generate, attach, and display the alternate renditions. This interface is instantiated during the file creation process if the related
SPFFileType indicates that it supports the generation of alternate renditions.
ISPFAlternateRenditionInfo interface
This is a link interface.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFAlternateRenditionApplication
The application used to create the alternate rendition.
Code
SPFAlternateRenditionPath
This is a separated list of file types through which the file is converted to the relevant type.
For example, in SmartPlant Foundation a Word file is converted through postscript using
FT_WordFile/FT_PSFile/FT_PDFFile
Code
ISPFAlternateRenditionSchedulerTask interface
This interface schedules the generation of alternate renditions.
The following properties are found on this interface:
Name
Description
Set on
SPFSchTskAlternateRenditionFileType

ISPFFile interface
See File interface model.
ISPFFileType interface
See File interface model.
ISPFMergedRendition interface
This interface implies the ISPFFile interface definition and is realized by the SPFAlternateRendition class definition. This interface is instantiated only if a merged PDF file is related to a parent object using
ISPFMergedRenditionsComposition through the SPFMergedRenditionComposition relationship definition where End1 is ISPFMergedRenditionsComposition (the parent object) and End2 is ISPFMergedRendition (the merged
PDF file).
SPFMergedRenditionComposition relationship definition
This relationship definition links the parent object (ISPFMergedRenditionsComposition) to the merged alternate rendition (ISPFMergedRendition).
ISPFMergedRenditionsComposition interface
This interface is realized by the class definition of any selected objects containing files for merged PDF generation. This interface is instantiated only if an object is selected for merged PDF generation and the merged PDF file is related to
the object through the SPFMergedRenditionComposition relationship definition where End1 is ISPFMergedRenditionsComposition (the parent object) and End2 is ISPFMergedRendition (the merged PDF file).
Summary of Delivered Core Document Model
This section describes the components of the document management model delivered by the core configuration. This core is common to all SmartPlant Foundation models, whether they are used for stand-alone, interactive document
management, or complete SmartPlant® Enterprise integration.
In some cases, components of the other models are also described; when this happens, the model a component belongs to is clearly stated.
Vaults
To see a list of the vaults configured for your environment, set your query scope to Scope Not Set (Configuration Top), and click Find > Administration > Vault.
Alternate rendition file type path
The alternate rendition path is used in case of multi-step process of alternate rendition. The core delivered model generates PDF files for Word files using a multi-step process. Initially, Word is converted into PS, and then the PS is
converted into PDF. For example, the alternate rendition path is as follows:
FT_WordFile/FT_PSFile/FT_PDFFile
Revision schemes
For a list of the revision schemes that are configured for your environment, click Find > Administration > Revision Scheme. View the details of a revision scheme to see the supported sequence.
Form sections
A number of sections are delivered out of the box, based on standard interfaces and purposes. To see the base form sections in your system, click Find > Administration > Form Section. You can view details about the delivered sections
by right-clicking a section, and clicking Details.
Printing
Printing in the context of SmartPlant Foundation encompasses many concepts.
Printing all files attached to a design document
Printing to a certain printer or application based on file type
Printing locally or batch printing
Printing on a network
Scheduling a printing job
Creating alternate renditions such as .pdf
Specifying watermarks and labels on a printed file
Printing in SmartPlant Markup Plus
Batch printing, local printing, and Office files
Batch printing is a scheduled operation, whereby the Scheduler retrieves the files to be printed. Printers, printer groups, and printing relationships must be set up before scheduling batch printing. Batch printing is configured using the
FileBatchPrint method. Batch printing can also be configured as a workflow. Refer to How to Configure the Workflow Model for more information.
If the associated printing application is configured to be SmartPlant Markup Plus ("dmredl"), batch printing of Microsoft Office files uses view renditions (CSFs). If a view rendition does not exist, the software generates one and then prints it.
Batch printing of Office files does not occur if view rendition generation fails. For more information, see Conversion and batch printing errors.
 To print a document with markups or reference files attached, you must configure the BatchPrint client API to print documents with markups or reference files. For more information, see BatchPrint client API.
Local printing sends files directly to a selected local printer or plotter and is configured using the FileLocalPrint method.
If the default printing application is configured to be SmartPlant Markup Plus ("dmredl"), local printing of Microsoft Office files requires view renditions (CSFs). If a view rendition does not exist, printing does not occur. A message is
displayed to indicate the file(s) that failed to print, and a request to generate the view rendition is submitted in the background. For more information, see View rendition error when printing Microsoft Office files locally.
For a discussion of strategies for watermarks and labels, refer to How to Configure the Document Schema.
Enable print options on the Print Options dialog box
By default, some options are unavailable on the Print Options dialog box. Do the following to enable these options.
1. Log on to the Desktop Client as a user with administrative privileges.
2. Find the FileBatchPrint method.
3. Right-click the method, and select Update.
4. Select the print options you want to enable under Method details.
For example, select the Print markups check box to enable Print with markups and Print markups in color.
Print scheduler task
The print scheduler handles the printing of files at a specific date and time.
Configure batch printing
Configuring batch printing involves performing several steps on the SmartPlant Foundation application server as well as in the Desktop Client.
The following work process diagram illustrates how to set up and configure batch printing:
 Click a box in the diagram to go to the associated procedure.
Configure the SPF Remote Services user
The SPF Remote Services user must be added to the Administrators group so that this user has the necessary privileges to start an application (such as SmartPlant Markup Plus) during batch printing.
If the defaults were selected in the Server Manager when the site was created, you can add the SPF Remote Services user to the Administrators group. For more information, see Adding SPFRemoteServices to the Administrators
group. If SmartPlant Foundation was configured manually, you must first determine the user name and password for SPF Remote Services.
At least one printer must be installed on the server for batch printing to work.
Determine the user name for the SPF Remote Services user
1. Log in to the server as a user with administrator privileges.
2. Click Start > Administrative Tools > Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager.
3. Expand the server name, and click Sites > Default Web Site.
4. Select [Sitename]RemoteSvc in the Default Web Site tree.
5. Click Basic Settings to display the Edit Application dialog box.
6. Write down the value in the Application pool box.
7. Click Cancel.
8. In the tree on the left, click Application Pools.
9. Select the application pool name. This value was determined in the previous steps.
10. Click Advanced Settings to display the Advanced Settings dialog box.
11. Write down the value found in the Identity box in the Process Model section. This value will be the user name.
12. Click Cancel.
13. Close Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager.
Determine the password for the SPF Remote Services user
1. Open Windows Explorer, and navigate to the following folder:
C:\Program Files (x86)\SmartPlant\Foundation\SPFServerManager\UsersAndPermissionsScripts\
2. Right-click CreateSiteScriptWithNewUserIIS6.bat and open the file using Notepad.
3. In the opened file, locate and confirm the defined password. This is the default password used when the new site was created and is typically #1#2#3#4#a#b#c. Write down the password.
 If the default password is different from #1#2#3#4#a#b#c, write down the password. The user password must match what is used for the application pool identity.
4. Close Notepad.
Add the SPF Remote Services user to the Administrators group
1. Click Start > Administrative Tools > Server Manager.
2. Click and expand the Configuration > Local Users and Groups > Groups tree.
3. Right-click Administrators.
4. Click Add to Group.
5. Click Add on the Administrators Properties dialog box.
6. On the Select Users dialog box, type hostname\username in the Enter the object names to select box.
The host name is the name of the server.
The user name was found in the Determine the user name for the SPF Remote Services user section above.
7. Click Check Names.
8. Click OK on the Select Users dialog box.
9. Click OK on the Administrators Properties dialog box.
10. Log off the server.
Configure Microsoft Office
Microsoft Office must be configured so that our third-party software can convert Office files to the proper format for batch printing.
1. Log in to the server with the user name and password determined in Configure the SPF Remote Services user. By default, the user name and password are SPFRemoteServices #1#2#3#4#a#b#c.
2. Open Microsoft Word or Microsoft Excel.
3. Complete the Microsoft Office system dialog boxes and other activation dialog boxes.
Give users access to the SPF Remote Services temporary folder
All SmartPlant Foundation users need access to this temporary folder because files are created and deleted in this folder during the printing process.
1. Log in to the server with the user name and password determined in Configure the SPF Remote Services user. By default, the user name and password are SPFRemoteServices #1#2#3#4#a#b#c.
2. In Windows Explorer, type %TEMP%.
3. Navigate up a level.
4. Right-click the Temp folder, and select Properties.
5. Click the Security tab.
6. Click Edit.
7. Select SPFUsers in the Group or user names list on the Permissions for Temp dialog box.
 If the SPFUsers group is not in the list, follow this procedure to add it.
a. Click Add.
b. On the Select Users dialog box, type servername\SPFUsers in the Enter the object names to select box.
c. Click OK.
8. Select the Modify check box.
9. Click OK.
10. Log off the server.
Create printers
1. Click File > New > Administration > Printer in the Desktop Client.
2. Type a name for the printer.
3. In the Command name box, type the name of the printer to be used (for example, SmartPlant PDF Converter NNN (where NNN denotes the version number)).
 This printer can be a local printer on the SmartPlant Foundation server, or it can be a shared network printer. For a local printer, the name you type in the Command name box must match the printer name in the Control
Panel.
For a shared network printer, type the UNC path to the printer, for example, \\printserver\printqueue. In addition, the user running SPFRemoteServices must be granted print access to the shared network printer.
4. Select the Offline indicator check box if you want an indication when the printer is off-line.
5. Click Apply.
6. Repeat these steps for each printer in the printer group.
7. When all the printers have been created, click Finish.
Create a printer group
1. Click File > New > Administration > Printer Group in the Desktop Client.
2. Type a name for the printer group.
3. In the URL box, type the address of the print service. For example, type http://<Computer Name>/<RemoteServices virtual dir>/PrintService.asmx.
 The URL points to the location of the SPFRemoteServices folder.
Create an organization
SmartPlant Foundation users can be grouped together into organizations. Relationships between organizations and printer groups control access to printers, so you must have at least one organization to configure printing.
1. Click File > New > Organization / Support Items > External Company.
 You must have document controller or administrator role privileges to access the Organization / Support Items submenu.
 You can also create departments, offices, manufacturers, and suppliers. Any of these items can be used to configure printing.
2. Fill in the required options, indicated by an asterisk (*).
3. Click Finish.
Create printing relationships
Once you have created the components used in printing in the SmartPlant Foundation database, you must create relationships between them. The software uses these relationships to determine which printers to use during the batch
printing process.
Create the printer-to-printer group relationship
1. In the Desktop Client, drag the printer group to the printer. The New Relationship form is displayed.
2. Click OK to create the relationship.
Create the organization-to-printer group relationship
1. Click Find > Organization / Support Items > All Organizations to display all the organizations in the Desktop Client.
2. Drag an organization to the printer group. The New Relationship form is displayed.
 An organization can be an external company, department, office, manufacturer, or supplier.
3. Click OK to create the relationship.
Create the file type-to-printer relationship
 This procedure to create the file type-to-printer relationship is optional. If there is no associated print program on the file type-to-printer relationship, the software uses the default application on the file type when batch printing.
1. In the Desktop Client, drag the file type to the printer. The New Relationship form is displayed.
2. Click Maintain Attributes. The Create form is displayed.
3. In the Associated print program box, type the printer application details (for example, dmredl.exe -p "$FILENAME" -q "$PRINTER").
The following relationships have now been configured:
User -> Organization -> Printer Group(s) -> Printer(s) -> File Type(s)
Configure file dimensions extraction for Hexagon AB file types
 File dimensions will not be extracted for non-Hexagon AB file types, even if you select the File dimensions to be extracted check box.
File dimensions extraction retrieves the height, width, and units of measurement for a Hexagon AB file type. To retrieve the file dimensions, do the following:
1. Check if the interface ISPFFileInfo is realized by the class definition of the Hexagon AB file type to be printed. If it is not, add the interface to the corresponding class definition.
2. Find the Hexagon AB file type to be printed.
3. Right-click the Hexagon AB file type and select Update.
4. Select the File dimensions to be extracted check box.
Test the batch printing configuration
1. Create a document and attach several files to it.
2. In the Desktop Client, right-click the document and click Files > Print to open the Batch Print Files dialog box.
The files and associated printers are pre-selected.
The files are shown under a specific printer if a printer-to-file type relationship exists.
If a file is related to more than one printer, the first accessible printer on the relationship is used to populate the dialog box.
If a file is now related to any accessible printer, the software checks for the last used printer in the client settings file.
