New Activity Workpack Template dialog box
Create activities
Activity Items (Vendor Docs) relate to the parent Activity. Typically, these would be documents that get sent to and received from Suppliers. Each time there would be a status code on the document.
The parent Activity is the object that gets Registered and Progressed. It uses the same Hierarchy as Design Progress. Activities are registered to Activity Workpacks.
The parent Activity’s progress is recalculated every time one of its Activity Items is updated or an item is added or removed.
The aggregation of the child Activity Items forms the basis of the Activity’s progress.
Activity lifecycle
A typical lifecycle of an activity progressed deliverable is as follows:
1. Deliverable metadata is created in the system and the deliverable is related to the required activity. The deliverable’s metadata will include a due date, that is, the date when the physical document is needed by.
2. Document for the deliverable is received and checked-in. The Actual Receipt Date of the deliverable is updated.
3. The document is internally reviewed after which a Return Status Code will be assigned to the deliverable.
4. The document is sent issued back to its originator (on a transmittal) and the Actual Return Date and Planned Due Date of the deliverable is set.
Create an activity
1. Click File > New > Progress > Activity in the Desktop Client.
2. Type a name and description for activity.
3. Set the Weighting and the Activity details (see following table).
4. Click Finish.
Option
Description
Weighting
The weighting of the activity in relation to its peers. For example, the man hour estimates could be used.
Expected number of deliverables
The expected total number of deliverables.
Overrides the actual number of deliverables currently on the activity (unless it is a greater number).
Deliverable type
Enumerated extensible list that can configured by the users to provide more details of the activity.
Contract
The top hierarchy level of the deliverable.
Area
The second hierarchy level of the deliverable.
Unit
The third hierarchy level of the deliverable.
Create design documents
A deliverable is assigned to an appropriate workpack based upon matching each of the hierarchy levels with one of its properties. This assignment of a deliverable to a workpack occurs during the registration of the deliverable.
Create a design document
When a new design document is created, to enable it to be registered for progress, you must:
1. Select the Contract.
2. Select the Functional area and unit.
3. Type a Weighting value.
Register a deliverable with a workpack
1. Find the deliverable that you want to associate with a workpack.
2. Right-click on the deliverable, and click Register for Progress.
3. In the Progress section of the Details tab, view the weighting for the deliverable.
 The Register for Progress dialog box differs slightly for Design and Activity deliverables.
4. In the Man hours section, indicate the number of man hours that have been budgeted for the completion of this deliverable and the number of man hours that will be required to complete it. These figures are maintained based on the
allocated man hours for the workpack and the weighting and level of completion of the deliverable.
5. On the Steps tab, review the Options selected for the deliverable. If you want to enable the downstream forecasting or calculation override features for this deliverable, make sure the appropriate check box is selected.
 These check boxes are available only if those features have been selected as available for the workpack when it was created.
Register a tag for progress
To register a tag for progress:
Create the tag class (or expand an existing one), and then make the class realize the interfaces that can be progressed. These interfaces are:
ISPFPrgProgressItem plus either ISPFPrgDesignItem or ISPFPrgActivity.
Plan dates must be set during registration. However, only the plan date for the first production step can be entered by the user; the remaining plan dates are calculated based upon the step durations.
Actual dates and progress percentages are not enterable.
Only the forecast date for the first production step can be entered by the user; the remaining forecast dates are calculated based upon the step durations.
When registering multiple activities, they must be of the same deliverable type, and hence share the same review and return periods, and therefore use common step durations.
Update the progress of a deliverable
Find the deliverable for which you want to update the progress.
1. Right-click on the deliverable, and click Update Progress.
2. On the Details tab of the Update Progress dialog box, review the information in the Progress section, and update it as necessary.
 The Last updated field indicates the date on which the displayed data was generated. Run a manual rollup to recalculate that data with the latest available.
3. In the Man hours section, update the number of man hours that have been budgeted for the completion of this deliverable or the number of man hours that will be required to complete it.
4. On the Steps tab, review the dates and statuses provided for each step defined for the workpack. Provide or modify dates as necessary.
 Actual dates can be the current or a previous date. You cannot enter a future date in an actual field.
Activity progress status codes
In activity progress, each step except the first one has a return status code (or status code). A step's status will move from pending or overdue to complete when the activity deliverable (or document) has met the set status code. It can also
move back to pending or overdue if the document's status code changes or if a document is added to the workpack.
The following color codes represent the step statuses. Click Show color keys to display the color keys on the UpdateProgress dialog box.
For cells that display dates, the colors are as follows. These are the cell colors.
Dark Green
The date that was entered is before the start date of the plan detail.
Red
The date that was entered is after the end date of the plan detail.
For the Step name cells, the colors represent the status of each step, and are as follows. These are the header colors.
Red
The step has not been completed yet, and the plan date is past the step's due
date.
Beige
The step has not reached the pending stage. For example, if the value of the
Pending Days option has been set to five days, the step will be Not due if the
current date is more than five days away from when this step needs to be
completed. The Pending Days value is set on the Progress Options dialog.
Yellow
The calendar date is within the pending days value. For example, if Pending
Days is five, then the current calendar date is five or fewer days away from
when the step needs to be completed. The Pending Days value is set on the
Progress Options dialog.
Light Green
The step has been completed.
Update activity item dates
You can update planned and forecast dates for a deliverable related to an activity workpack.
Right-click a deliverable, and then click Manage Activity Item Dates. You can use the Manage Activity Item Dates dialog box to set the dates manually or use the activity workpack planned dates to calculate them.
Actual receipt date - The date the deliverable was received from the supplier.
Planned return date - The date the deliverable will be returned, based on the schedule defined by the deliverable review periods.
Actual return date - The date the deliverable was actually returned to the supplier.
Due date - The date the deliverable is due back from the supplier, based on the schedule defined by the deliverable return periods.
Promised date - The date the supplier promised to return the deliverable. This is not a mandatory field, and can be used as required.
Status code - The status code, or return code, for the deliverable. Status codes can represent statuses such as rejected or approved, and determine whether the deliverable can be moved to the next step in the workpack.
Set Planned Return Date - Sets the planned return date for the deliverable based on the schedule of the activity workpack. This date equals the receipt date + internal and external review periods configured for that deliverable type and
status.
Set Due Date - Sets the due date for the deliverable based on the schedule of the activity workpack. This date equals the return date + the return period configured for that deliverable type and status.
Update progress for multiple deliverables
1. Select the deliverables for which you want to update the progress.
2. Right-click, and select Update Progress.
 Use the options on the View menu of the Multiple Deliverable Progress Update dialog box to determine just what information appears in the table.
3. Activate the check box beside each deliverable for which you want to make the same change, and make a change to a value for one of those deliverables. Your change is carried over to all other deliverables with an active check box.
Unregister a deliverable from a workpack
1. Find the deliverable that you want to unregister from progress.
2. Right-click on the deliverable, and click Unregister with Progress.
3. On the confirmation dialog that appears, click Yes to disassociate the deliverable.
Configure replan
Replanning provides the ability to revise plan dates for workpacks and registered progress items. Plan dates entered during replan mode are reviewed and are only applied when a replan is accepted.
A replan is can be initiated at any configuration and only one replan can be active for each configuration.
Run a replan
1. Click Progress > Replan > Initiate Replan in the Desktop Client. The Replan Initiated form is displayed.
The client gets the properties for the current hierarchy by calling the ProgressGetProgressHierarchy server API.
2. Once the form has been completed the client calls the ProgressInitiateReplan Server API, which will set any active workpacks for the selected level into replan mode. Every deliverable in all workpacks with the same hierarchy is
placed in replan mode (that is, only the replan dates can be modified).
3. Click OK to confirm.
Replan process
For each affected workpack, replan will:
Set the replan indicator on the workpack to true.
If the deliverables frozen indicator is set to true, it is set to false.
If present, the forecast start date is copied to the replan start date, else the plan start date is copied.
If present, the forecast end date is copied to the replan end date, else the plan end date is copied.
If present, the construction start date is copied to the replan construction start date.
If present, the construction end date is copied to the replan construction end date.
For each production step for each affected deliverable:
if the production step has an actual date, then copy it to the replan start date, else
if the production step has a forecast date, then copy it to the replan start date, else
copy the production step’s plan date to the replan date
Change the replan dates
To change the replan dates for a deliverable:
1. While in replan mode, right-click the deliverable in the Desktop Client, and click Update Progress in the shortcut menu.
2. Select the replan dates.
The default replan dates are based upon the actual dates provided for steps. If an actual date does not exist for a step, the replan date will be based on the forecast date of the step. If a step has no forecast date, the replan date is
based on the plan date.
These dates must fall within their Replan Start and End dates for the workpack.
The replan dates do not have to obey the time string that is set for the current deliverable. When the replan is accepted, the system will create a new time string if necessary.
Accept the replan
Dates entered during replan mode are only applied when a replan is accepted.
1. While in replan mode, click Progress > Replan > Accept Replan in the Desktop Client.
2. Click OK to accept the replan.
When a replan is accepted, the following changes are made to the workpack details:
The Plan Start and Plan End dates for the workpack are replaced with the Replan Start and Replan End dates.
The Construction Start and Construction End dates are replaced with the Replan Construction Start and Replan Construction End dates.
The Replan Start, Replan End, Replan Construction Start, and Replan Construction End date boxes on the Plan Details tab of the Design Workpack Create/Update dialog box are cleared.  
For each deliverable in the workpack, the following changes are made:
The plan dates for each step are replaced with the replan dates.
The forecast dates and replan dates for each step are cleared.
The Actual dates for each step are not affected.
If a deliverable's new plan dates do not correspond to an available time string, a new time string will be created and related to it by the Accept Replan process. The new timestring will also be available on the appropriate workpack. It
is named with a prefix of "RP" and an incremental number.
Cancel the replan
Plan dates entered during replan mode are not applied until the replan is accepted.
1. To cancel, while in replan mode, click Progress > Replan > Cancel Replan in the Desktop Client.
2. Click OK to cancel the replan.
View a replan
Click Progress > Replan > View Replan.
The values in the Area and Unit fields indicate the hierarchy level of the current replan.
Configure rollups
The Run Rollup routine provides the ability to calculate and store aggregated progress at each level of the progress reporting hierarchy.
The Run Rollup command uses the information defined for every deliverable in every workpack that is included in the rollup to calculate the current level of completion of each workpack (when applicable) and consequently the level of
completion of that particular configuration.
Once the roll-up has been performed, the information is stored for that particular cut-off date, and the information is available for viewing through the View Progress user interface.
 Progress is calculated at every level of the hierarchy, including every deliverable associated with every workpack, so for larger sets of data, this process may take some time.
Run rollup
Click Progress > Run Rollup in the Desktop Client.
Only one progress rollup process can be invoked for a given configuration at a time.
Progress is calculated at every level of the hierarchy, including every deliverable associated with every workpack, so for larger sets of data, this process may take some time.
The progress rollup process is designed to be invoked for a specific configuration using the SPFProgress command line routine, or from the scheduler. A Server API called ProgressRollup is called for a specified configuration object (such
as a project) and will invoke the ISPFConfiguration.ProgressRollup method definition.
The progress rollup process writes error, warning, and information messages to an output file to log all stages of its progression through the routine.
The error log is called ProgressRollup_<date>.log where date is the date the rollup was run. The path for file is configurable depending on the SmartPlant Foundation configuration settings, (such as C:\temp).
The progress rollup process raises events at key stages. If required, event definitions and corresponding subscription definitions can be created that will automatically notify an administrator when certain events have been raised. For
example, it would be possible to notify the administrator on failure or completion of the process.
Ad hoc progress
The system allows for the rolling up of progress on an ad hoc basis. This allows project implementations to calculate rolled up progress for specified reporting levels.
Ad hoc progress calculations are based upon the current progress figures for specified deliverables. That is, the progress figures for the specified deliverables will not be recalculated.
A server API called ProgressAdhocReport is provided for use by project implementations. The API will expect as input xml that lists progress items in their desired levels and will output xml containing the results.
Progress Model
This section describes the Progress data model in detail. For information on how to configure the core model, see How to Configure the Progress Model.
Progress reporting hierarchy
The progress hierarchy is used to provide the project structure for which deliverables are grouped (workpacks) and the levels to which progress is calculated, rolled up, and viewed. Projects may require differing reporting structures and
hence a system-wide structure may be inappropriate.
For example, the delivered model hierarchy is based on the contract, area, and unit to which the deliverable is related.
Each progress hierarchy is defined on a hierarchy interface and these are optionally realized by the configuration classes such as Plant and Project. When a plant or project is created, one of these optional hierarchy interfaces is selected
and instantiated and this identifies the hierarchy for that configuration.
When creating a workpack or template, the hierarchy interface that is relevant to the current create configuration (for example, project) will be instantiated on the workpack and will include a value for each of the interface’s properties.
There are two ways to set the values to each property definition on the hierarchy interface:
1. Scope the property as an enumerated list type.
2. Cater for property definitions that must present a list of values that correspond to instantiated objects (for example, area and unit). To do this, a view definition must be created with the same UID as the property definition with a suffix
of ‘Values’. The first property in the view definition will be used to provide the list of values.
When an item is registered for progress, it is necessary to extract the values for the item that match each of the properties in the progress hierarchy for the current create configuration. Each value may represent one of the item’s properties
or a property on a related object. To retrieve this data, the system needs to expand a pre-defined view definition for the item. To allow for the possibility that a workpack may contain different progressable classes, it is necessary to resolve a
relevant view definition for each class to navigate to the appropriate object(s) to obtain the values for each hierarchy property.
To do this, the system will search for all view definitions whose name is prefixed with the name as selected hierarchy interface. The starting interface of each graph definition that comprise the resultant view definitions is compared against
the realized interfaces of the item to be registered. The view definition that has a matching starting interface is selected and expanded for the progress item to extract hierarchy values. Once the hierarchy values for the deliverable have
been extracted, they can be matched against the available ‘active’ workpacks to find a match.
For example:
If three objects (a document, a tag and a purchase order) are registered for progress and each item's properties are stored in different ways, then each would require a different view definition to be expanded to obtain the properties. Then:
If the document properties are stored on the object itself - then ISPFPrgHierarchyH1_DesignDoc would be a view definition to the properties.
If the tag has a relationship to its properties- then ISPFPrgHierarchyH1_Tag would be a view definition to get the names of the related properties.
If the purchase order has a relationship to an object where the properties are stored - then ISPFPrgHierarchyH1_Purchase would be a view definition to the object where the properties are stored.
A special command is available to instantiate the required hierarchy interfaces on the plants and projects. This is the 'Set Hierarchy' pull down menu option.
When instantiating these optional interfaces, the following rules must be applied:
Only one workpack and one workpack template hierarchy interface to be instantiated for each configuration item.
If at least one workpack has been created at a particular configuration, then the hierarchy interface cannot be deleted, amended, or added for the corresponding configuration item.
The configuration item must have workpack hierarchy interface instantiated before a workpack can be created for that configuration. Similarly, the configuration item must have workpack template hierarchy interface instantiated
before a workpack template can be created for that configuration.
Any class definition that represents a configuration item must realize any workpack or workpack template hierarchy interface definitions that are required for selection.
Progress reporting hierarchy model
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these interfaces are given in the sections that follow.
ISPFPrgHierarchy interface
This interface is used internally to identify all the progress hierarchy interfaces.
ISPFPrgWorkpackHierarchy interface
This identifies the hierarchy for workpacks and is key to progress functionality. It is implied by each of the workpack hierarchy interfaces configured by the administrator and so identifies these as the hierarchy definitions for the workpacks.
ISPFPrgWorkpackH1 interface
This is a sample progress hierarchy interface for workpacks and is delivered with the core progress model. It is instantiated by the workpack class and plants and projects.
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
Contract
The top level of the hierarchy for which the workpack will be available.
Code
Area
The sub level of the hierarchy for which the workpack will be available.
Code
Unit
The second sub level of the hierarchy for which the workpack will be available.
Code
The properties are not set on the plant and project objects; the interface is instantiated to identify the progress hierarchy being used. The properties are set on the workpack objects only.
ISPFPrgWorkpack interface
This interface is realized by the workpack.
Functionality Configured on ISPFPrgProgressItem
The commands Freeze Plan Dates, Redistribute Man Hours, Set Progress Updated, and Unfreeze Plan Dates are configured on this interface.
ISPFPrgWorkpackTemplateHierarchy interface
This identifies the hierarchy for workpack templates and is key to progress functionality. It is implied by each of the workpack template hierarchy interfaces configured by the administrator and so identifies these as the hierarchy definitions
for the workpack templates.
ISPFPrgWorkpackTemplateH1 interface
This is a sample progress hierarchy interface for workpack templates and is delivered with the core progress model. It is used to categorize templates. 
The following properties are found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
Contract
The top level of the hierarchy for which the workpack will be available.
Code
ISPFConfigurationItem interface
This interface is realized by all configuration items such as Plants and Projects. It is not specific to the progress model.
The ISPFConfigurationItem interface will optionally imply each of the available hierarchy interfaces. This will allow one of the hierarchy interfaces to be selected for each configuration item (such as plant and project).
Progress workpacks and templates
It is possible to create templates which will provide the ability to quickly create workpacks.
Template definitions are carried out in the same way as workpack definitions (see Design workpacks) with the following exceptions:
Unlike a workpack which is identified by its progress reporting hierarchy values, templates will be identified by up to five properties are categorized. This may or may not match all or a portion of the progress reporting hierarchy.
Templates do not have plan details.
The Allocated Weighting, Override Allocated Weighting, In Replan Mode, Progress Updated, Include in rollup, and Deliverable Plan Frozen properties found on workpacks are not relevant to templates.
Templates can only be created for a specific configuration.
Workpack structure
The basic structure of the difference between workpacks and workpack templates is shown in the following diagram.
Activity workpack templates
Activity workpack templates provide the ability to pre-define unique sets of production steps and weightings that can be used to quickly create activity workpacks. Activity workpack template definitions do not have plan detail and can only
be created for a specific configuration.
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these classes are given in the sections that follow.
SPFPrgActivityWorkpackTemplate class
This is the Activity Workpack Template class used to define template steps and durations to assist in the definition of activity workpack templates.
ISPFPrgActivityWorkpackTemplate interface
This is the primary interface of the Activity Workpack Template class.
Functionality configured on ISPFPrgActivityWorkpackTemplate
The command Create Workpack is available on this interface.
ISPFPrgWorkpackTemplate interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgWorkpackDef interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgWorkpackStepComposition interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgWorkpackStep interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgActivityWorkpackStep interface
This contains the step status code.
The following property is found on this interface.
Name
Description
Set on
SPFPrgActivityStepReturnStatusCodes
The return status code(s) that will complete this step
Create/Update Activity Workpack Template form
Activity workpacks
Activity workpacks are used to progress activities that manage the progress of sets of deliverables, whereas design workpacks manage the progress of individual deliverables.
Activity workpacks are identified by their progress reporting hierarchy and include plan details. All the steps of an activity workpack must have a status return code (except for the first step). Activity workpack steps are dynamic and activity
items do not have to follow them in a precise sequence (and may even move back through the steps).   
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these classes are given in the sections that follow.
SPFPrgActivityWorkpack class
This is the Activity Workpack class used to group activities for progress.
ISPFPrgActivityWorkpack interface
This is the primary interface for the SPFPrgActivityWorkpack class.
ISPFPrgWorkpack interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgWorkpackPlanDetail interface
See the Design workpacks description.
ISPFPrgWorkpackDef interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgWorkpackStepComposition interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgWorkpackStep interface
See the Design workpack templates description.
ISPFPrgActivityWorkpackStep interface
See the Activity workpack templates description.
Design workpack templates
Design workpack templates provide the ability to pre-define unique sets of production steps and weightings that can be used to quickly create design workpacks. Design workpack template definitions do not have plan detail and can only
be created for a specific configuration.
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these classes are given in the sections that follow.
SPFPrgDesignWorkpackTemplate class
This is the Design Workpack Template class used to define templates of workpack steps and durations to assist in the definition of design workpacks. It stores the composition of the workpack steps and time strings but does not hold the
man hours.
ISPFPrgDesignWorkpackTemplate interface
This is the primary interface for the SPFPrgDesignWorkpackTemplate class.

The command Create Design Workpack is available on this interface.
ISPFPrgWorkpackTemplate interface
This is implied by ISPFPrgDesignWorkpackTemplate and is the interface where most of the progress workpack template functionality is written.

Design workpacks
Design workpacks are identified by their progress reporting hierarchy and include plan details. Design workpacks may be linked to one or more events, for example, the sign-off of a deliverable at specified revisions. Design workpack steps
are linear, and the deliverable must move through all the steps in sequence.
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these classes are given in the sections that follow.
SPFPrgDesignWorkpack class
This is the Design Workpack class used for grouping design deliverables. It stores the composition of the workpack steps and time strings and also man hour and date window information.
ISPFPrgDesignWorkpack interface
This is the primary interface for the SPFPrgDesignWorkpack class.

Progress deliverables
Progress Items (that is, deliverables) are objects that realize the ISPFPrgProgressItem interface and either the ISPFPrgDesignItem or ISPFPrgActivity interface. Only progress items can be registered for progress.
When a progress item is registered for progress, the optionally implied ISPFPrgRegisteredItem interface is instantiated and a relationship between the progress item and the workpack is created (between the ISPFPrgRegisteredItem and
ISPFPrgWorkpack interfaces). For each workpack step of the target workpack, an object with class definition SPFPrgItemStep is created. Relationships between these item step objects to workpack steps and to the progress item are
created.
Two types of deliverables are implemented:
Design deliverables - the individual deliverables are tracked.
Activity deliverables - sets of deliverables are tracked together.
Design deliverables
Design deliverables (such as design documents and tags) are progressed as a single object and its progress is tracked linearly (that is, it has sequential steps).
In design deliverable progress, a step may be linked to an event (for example, the sign-off of a deliverable at specified revisions or the issue of a deliverable on a transmittal for a specified reason).
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these interfaces are given in the sections that follow.
SPFDesignDocMaster class
A design deliverable is identified by realizing the ISPFPrgDesignItem and ISPFPrgProgressItem interfaces and optionally realizing the ISPFPrgRegisteredItem interfaces. Progress items realizing the ISPFPrgDesignItem interface can only
be associated to workpacks realizing the ISPFPrgDesignWorkpack interface.
SPFDesignDocMaster is one of the delivered progressable document classes; however, any class can be a progress item if it realizes these interfaces.
To enable a design document to be registered for progress, it must be allocated a weighting. (This can be done when it is created or later.)
The weighting field on the design document Create/Update form is only available if progress is switched on (by having a hierarchy selected) in the current configuration.
When the object is allocated, a weighting on the following interfaces are instantiated:
ISPFPrgDesignItem
ISPFPrgProgressItem
When the object is registered for progress, the ISPFPrgRegisteredItem interface is instantiated.
ISPFPrgWorkpack interface
See Progress workpacks and templates description.
SPFPrgWorkpackRegisteredWorkpack relationship definition
This relates the registered deliverable to its workpack.

This interface needs to be instantiated by any item that is to be registered for progress.

workpack

ISPFPrgDesignItem interface
This interface is instantiated when the object is allocated a weighting and enables the object to be registered for design progress.
ISPFPrgRegisteredItem interface
This implies that the deliverable on the workpack is registered for progress.
It contains the properties on the deliverable which are set based on their parent workpack's corresponding properties. It also holds the item steps. When a document is registered, it gets one step for each step on the workpack.

Activity deliverables
Activity items are not progressed individually but are related to a parent object that is used to manage and track progress percentages for the group. This parent object is called an Activity deliverable.
Activity items are grouped into Activity deliverables using a relationship between the ISPFPrgActivity and ISPFActivityItem interfaces.
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these interfaces are given in the sections that follow.
SPFPrgActivity class definition
The SPFPrgActivity class is delivered as a default example activity, the object that optionally gets registered. This class can be any item that realizes the ISPFPrgActivity, and ISPFPrgProgressItem interfaces and optionally realizes the
ISPFPrgRegisteredItem interface.
The ISPFPrgActivity interface can therefore be realized by any class definitions (such as purchase order, supplier activity, and so forth) that represent activities whose progress must be monitored.
ISPFPrgActivity interface
This is set on the object. It is set at object creation time to enable the object to be registered for activity progress.
The following properties are found on this interface.

Set On

activity.

New/Update Activity form

Progress rollup
The progress rollup process provides the ability to calculate, store, and report accumulated progress at each of the different levels in the progress reporting hierarchy.
When progress rollup is run, the system creates:
A progress rollup object for every hierarchy level.
A workpack rollup object for each workpack in the system which summarizes the workpack progress.
Ad hoc progress
The system allows for the rolling up of progress on an ad hoc basis. This allows project implementations to calculate rolled up progress for specified reporting levels.
 Ad hoc progress calculations are based upon the current progress figures for specified deliverables. That is, the progress figures for the specified deliverables will not be recalculated; the software will only base its calculations on
the figures from the most recent rollup.
Progress rollup diagram
Details of the properties and functionality exposed by these interfaces are given in the sections that follow.
