CreateFDWObject - must be set to TRUE if the object other than the document part object should be created.
For example, SPFNFDWDocDiscipline is mapped to SDADocDiscipline by default by specifying the following:
Column
Value
Data CaptureClassDef
SPFNDocumentMaster
Data CaptureRelDef
SPFNFDWDocDiscipline
IsData CaptureRelDelDirection12
True (since SPFNFDWDocDiscipline has the document master interface, ISPFNDocumentMaster,
at End1)
FDWRelDef
SDADocDiscipline
FDWClassDef
SDADiscipline (the class definition of the object in the FDW domain at the other end of the
relationship)
IsFDWRelDefDirection12
True (since the ISDADocMasterStructure interface is on End1)
CreateFDWObject
False (since the discipline object is the same in Data Capture and the FDW domain)
Property mappings can be created between different document parts, but for relationship mapping, the Data Capture and FDW document parts specified in the mapping must be the same level: master, revision, or version.
Create a new work package
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create a new work package (modified in an update).
What are work packages?
Work packages are containers that can be transferred to the Facility Data Warehouse using the managed data tasks. Work packages content varies depending upon the location of both staging and approval domains, the details of which
are given below:
Location of staging and approval domains
Work packages content
Same site
Documents to be transferred.
Different sites
Documents, tags, and other business objects to be transferred using Data Validator.
What do I use work packages for?
You can use work packages to organize the data that you want to transfer before running the managed data task.
Using work packages, you can also do the following:
View the relationships of the documents.
Apply a revision scheme to the selected documents.
Create a new work package for managed data tasks
1. Log on to Data Capture Task Manager module.
2. Click Managed Data Tasks.
3. On the Work Packages page, click Create Work Packages 
.
4. Select a column set associated with document from the Filter by list.
5. Type the search keyword in the Filter Value box, and click Search.
6. Select one or more documents from the Data Capture Data pane, and click Export to FDW domain 
 to move the documents to the FDW Ready Documents pane.
You can also click View Document Relationships 
 to view the relationships of the document in the Resolve Relationships module.
Select one or more documents in the FDW Ready Documents pane, and click Configure Document Revision Scheme 
 to apply a revision scheme to the selected documents.
7. Click Save.
Create a new work package for Data Validator transfer
1. On the Work Packages page, click Create Work Packages 
.
2. Select the Create Work Package for Data Validator Transfer check box.
3. Select a business object from the Business Objects list.
4. Select a column set associated with document from the Filter by list.
5. Type the search keyword in the Filter Value box, and click Search.
6. Select one or more documents from the Data Capture Data pane, and click Export to FDW domain 
 to move the selected documents to the FDW Ready Documents pane.
You can also click View Document Relationships 
 to view the relationships of the document in the Resolve Relationships module.
Select one or more documents in the FDW Ready Documents pane, and click Configure Document Revision Scheme 
 to apply a revision scheme to the selected documents.
7. On the Tags tab, select one or more tags from the Data Capture Data pane, and click Export to FDW domain 
 to move the selected tags to the FDW Ready Documents pane.
8. On the Others tab, select one or more business objects from the Data Capture Data pane, and click Export to FDW domain 
 to move the selected business objects to the FDW Ready Documents pane.
9. Click Save.
View managed data tasks
1. Log on to Data Capture Task Manager module.
2. Click Managed Data Tasks.
3. On the Managed Data Tasks page, you can view all the managed document tasks.
Create a managed data task
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create a new managed data task (modified in an update).
What can I do with a managed data task?
You can perform the following tasks with a managed data task:
Transfer existing documents from the staging domain (Data Capture or Data Validator) to the approval domain (Facility Data Warehouse).
Alter revision schemes for documents.
Validate documents and tags before transferring data to the Facility Data Warehouse.
Generate data validation and export process reports.
 Before preparing to work with large amount of data, based on the size of the data it is recommended to configure the LicenseTimeoutSeconds property under Site Settings node in SmartPlant Foundation Server Manager. This
setting will prevent the license token to timeout, thereby allowing the session to retain.
Create a new managed data task to transfer documents without using Data Validator
 You can create managed data tasks to transfer any document without using Data Validator from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse. After the transfer, the corresponding documents in Data Capture are deleted.
1. Click Managed Data Tasks in the Data Capture Task Manager.
2. In the Managed Data Tasks page, click Create Managed Data Tasks 
.
 Properties and relationships of Data Capture documents can be mapped to properties and relationships of FDW documents. For more information, see Property and relationship mapping.
3. Type the description of the managed data task in the Description box.
4. Click Convert to Managed Documents (Internal).
5. Select a work package from the Work Package list. For more information on work packages, see Create a new work package.
6. To export previously exported documents, select the Include Exported Data check box.
7. To export only the latest version of the document, select the Export Only Latest Version check box.
8. Click OK to export data to the Facility Data Warehouse.
 To view validation details and transfer details on each document, click Validate Report and Transfer Report respectively.
 Data Capture transfers Data Capture documents to the Facility Data WareHouse domain and creates Facility Data Warehouse documents without applying the ENS definition. However, if you want to create the Facility Data
Warehouse documents with ENS applied, we recommend that you select Data Capture Document Transfer to FDW in the View Definition Groups list and HEX Managed Data Task in the Data Validator Job Definition list, delivered
with the product, along with import mappings and other requisite configurations to export the data using Data Validator. You can also customize your own document mappings using Data Validator. For more information on how to configure
the document mappings using Data Validator, see Export objects configured with ENS in the target system.
Create a new managed data task to export data from Data Capture to FDW using Data Validator
How can I transfer documents from Data Capture to FDW efficiently using Data Validator?
To efficiently transfer documents from Data Capture to FDW while creating a Managed Data Task, select the following:
Data Capture Document Transfer to FDW in the View Definition Groups list - this view definition group contains the view definitions to export the data in CSV format.
HEX Managed Data Task in the Data Validator Job Definition list - this job definition contains the requisite import mappings, export mappings, rule sets, and target system details that will map the Data Capture documents to the
FDW documents.
 Edit the job definition to change the control file directory, user name, and target configuration. For more information, see Edit a Data Validator job definition.
 You can create managed data tasks to transfer any business object using Data Validator from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse.
1. Click Managed Data Tasks in the Data Capture Task Manager.
2. In the Managed Data Tasks page, click Create Managed Data Tasks 
.
3. Type the description of the managed data task in the Description box.
4. Click Export Data (Data Validator).
5. Select a work package from the Work Package list. For more information on work packages, see Create a new work package.
6. Select the Data Validator job definition from the Data Validator Job Definition list. For more information on Data Validator Job definitions, see Create Data Validator job definitions.
7. Select a view definition group from the View Definition Groups list.
8. To export previously exported documents, select the Include Exported Data check box.
9. To export only the latest version of the document, select the Export Only Latest Version check box.
10. Click OK to export data to the Facility Data Warehouse.
11. After the transfer, the corresponding object's status is not changed in Data Capture.
 Click Transfer Report to see transfer details of each document.
To validate data related to the task after the managed data task is created, click 
. After the data is validated, click Validation Report to see validation details for each document.
To export data related to the managed data task to the Facility Data Warehouse domain, click 
 after the managed data task is validated. After the data is exported, click Transfer Report to view transfer details of each document.
If you are trying to transfer business objects using Data Validator, you can transfer tags that correspond to specific documents directly without adding the tags to the work package. This can be done by creating a view definition group
with a document and tag pair that has same document interface. For more information, see Manage the view definition groups.
When you transfer documents using Data Validator, the corresponding attached files, navigation files and graphics map files for the documents are downloaded in the control file directory of the Data Validator Job definition, provided
the relationship definition information for the respective files exists in the view definition group.
The Data Validator supports the file relationships such as design files, markup files, navigation files, graphics map files, and alternate rendition files.
Check managed data task status
You can check the status of a managed data task in the Status column on the Managed Data Tasks page.
Managed data task creation statuses
The table below describes the status of the creation of managed data task.
Status
Description
Ready To Create
Managed data task has not been not created.
Creating
Managed data task is being created.
Created
Managed data task is created, and the task is ready to export business objects from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse.
Managed Document task statuses
When Data Capture and the Facility Data Warehouse are in the same system, a managed document task is created by the managed data task to transfer documents to the Facility Data Warehouse from Data Capture .The table below
describes the status of the managed document task:
Status
Description
Ready To Prepare Data
Managed data task has not been related to the documents in the work package.
Preparing Data
Managed data task is being relating to the documents in the work package.
Prepared Data
Managed data task related to the documents in the work package.
Ready to Validate
Data is ready for validation before transfer.
Validating
Data validation is in progress.
Passed Validation
Data validation completed.
Failed Validation
Data validation failed.
Ready to Export
Documents are ready for export to the Facility Data Warehouse .
Exporting
Documents are being transferred from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse.
Export Successful
Documents were successfully transferred from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse.
Export Failed
Documents failed to transfer from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse.
Business object transfer managed data task statuses
The table below describes the status of managed data tasks when Data Validator is used to transfer business objects from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse:
Why do I need to generate CSV files and control files?
Data Capture uses Data Validator to transfer data from source to target (Facility Data Warehouse). Data Validator reads the data that is to be transferred from the CSV and control files.
Status
Description
Generating CSV Files
CSV files are being generated at the path defined in the Data Validator job definition for transferring the business objects from Data Capture to the
Facility Data Warehouse.
Completed CSVFile Generation
CSV files were generated at the path defined in the Data Validator job definition.
Generating Control File
Control files are being generated at the path defined in the Data Validator job definition.
Completed ControlFile Generation
Control files were successfully generated at the path defined in the Data Validator job definition.
No CSV Files Generated
CSV files failed to generate at the path defined in the Data Validator job definition.
Data Validator Processing
Business objects are being exported to the Facility Data Warehouse using Data Validator.
Data Validator Processing Passed
Business objects are exported using Data Validator to the Facility Data Warehouse.
Data Validator Processing Failed
Business objects failed to export using Data Validator from Data Capture to the Facility Data Warehouse.
Use the Desktop Client to manage documents
The Desktop Client provides you with the following document management features:
Document revision and version control
Access control, including check in and check out
Check in a document using content extraction
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Check in a document using content extraction (modified in an update).
 Before preparing to work with large amount of data, based on the size of the data it is recommended to configure the LicenseTimeoutSeconds property under Site Settings node in SmartPlant Foundation Server Manager. This
setting will prevent the license token to timeout, thereby allowing the session to retain.
1. Find the FDW document, right-click it, and click Edit > CheckIn with Content Extraction.
2. In the CheckIn with Content Extraction dialog box, select a file and reader from Select Master File and Pre-Processor lists, respectively.
 To use the existing pre-processed files, select the Use Existing PreProcessed Content Files check box
3. Select the Match Tag Patterns check box to extract the tags that are defined in the Tag Discovery Patterns module.
 If the selected document contains a Smart 3D file, type the connection string in the OleDB Provider box to connect to the Microsoft Access database.
4. Select a template/template group from the Template Groups/Templates list.
 To replace the previous document version with the checked-in document, select the Replace Document Version check box.
5. Click CheckIn.
FDW tags are created without applying an ENS definition which is configured using the Desktop Client.
More Information
Provides additional information to help you use Data Capture.
Start or stop the Data Capture scheduler
You can use the Data Capture Task Manager Scheduler to start or stop the Data Capture schedulers when they are running scheduled tasks in the document discovery task, content discovery task, and the database domain discovery task
in the Data Capture Task Manager.
Why do I need to start the Data Capture Scheduler?
The Data Capture Scheduler must be running before you can:
Perform scheduled tasks from DDT (Document Discovery Task), CDT (Content Discovery Task) and DDDT (Database Domain Discovery Task).
Generate thumbnails for the files loaded in the product database.
1. Click Data Capture Task Manager to open the Data Capture Task Manager module.
2. Click Show Settings 
 .
3. In the Data Capture Task Manager Scheduler Settings window, do one of the following:
Click Start the Scheduler 
 to start the Data Capture Scheduler.
Click Stop the Scheduler 
 to stop the Data Capture Scheduler.
Click Restart the Scheduler 
 to re-start the scheduler.
4. Click Ok.
Preprocessed content XML file format
The following illustration shows a detailed view of the sections in a preprocessed content XML file.
1
The TagDomain node represents the domain name where the tag data extracted from this file is stored. In this
example, the tag data is stored in ABC domain. If a domain name is not available in the content file, all the tag data is
stored in the FDWTag domain.
2
Each _Tag node consists of tag data within it.
3
_Tag_Name represents the tag name and _Tag_Description represents the tag description.
4
Each _SPFN node represents the tag list property name. In this example, tag properties like Min Pressure, Max Temp,
Height, Length and Diameter are extracted.
5
Each _TitleBlock node consists of title block information. In this example, Attr1 is mapped with the Purchase Order
number.
The keywords must be prefixed with the underscore character for the application to extract them.
The title block information obtained from the file is stored on the document.
GraphicsMapFile format for different file types
The GraphicsMapFile.xml has the information for graphical navigation of an object. The following illustrations shows about the list of mandatory properties in the GraphicsMapFile.xml based on different file types:
For 3D file type:
The Map IDef represents the interface definition of the tag.
The UID represents an unique identifier of the tag to locate it in the system.
The Name represents the tag name.
The GOID represents the graphical co-ordinates for the tag in the 3D file.
For 2D/ drawing file type:
The Map IDef represents the interface definition of the tag.
The Name represents the tag name.
The GOID represents the graphical co-ordinates for the tag in the 2D or drawing file.
For PDF file type:
The GOID represents the graphical co-ordinates for the tag in the PDF file.
The Name represents the tag name.
The PageIndex represents the index value of the PDF.
The XMIN, YMIN, XMAX and YMAX represent the range values for the tag.
The Width represents the width of the PDF.
The Height represents the height of the PDF.
The UID represents an unique identifier of the tag to locate it in the system.
 UID is not a mandatory property.
Resolve reference files
The Resolve Reference File module allows you to view the reference file status and to resolve the reference relationships. You can only view the files that have unresolved reference files relationships.
 If you have only one reference file for a file, then it is automatically resolved by the application.
Search for documents
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Quality Control > Resolve Reference Files to open the Resolve Reference Files sub-module.
3. Type the name of the document that you want to search.
 You can type the entire name, part of the name, a wildcard character (%, *, or ?), or a combination of the name and wildcard character (for example, n%).
4. Click Search 
. The documents that match the criteria are displayed in a list view. The document title and the reference resolution status are displayed in the list view.
 A tick mark in the Reference Files Resolved column indicates that all the references relationships are resolved.
Filtering the objects in the list view
1. To filter the results based on a specific value in any of the columns, click Filter 
 at the top of that column.
2. Select the property or properties to display the items based on the criteria in the item list.
 You can further refine the results by selecting a condition value, such as Is equal to or Starts with.
3. Click Filter to display the filtered results automatically in the item list.
 By filtering on several properties, you can refine the results to get to a specific set of data.
Identify the reference files
1. On the Resolve Reference Files page, select one or more documents from the search results displayed in the list view.
2. Click Resolve Reference Files 
. The Identify Reference Files dialog box is displayed with the file information, which contains a comma separated list of files that are resolved, a comma separated list of files that are missing in
the database, and the files with more than one reference found in the database.
3. Select a reference file under each file name. You can click on the filename to view the actual file.
 Use expand 
 and collapse 
 to view the multiple reference files under each reference file node.
4. Click Save to save the relationship updates.
View reference files
1. On the Resolve Reference Files page, select a document from the search results displayed in the list view.
2. Click View Reference File Details 
. The Reference File Details dialog box displays the file name, reference file name, and the document name to which the reference file is attached.
 Use expand 
 and collapse 
 to view the reference file details.
Tag Pattern Extractor
The Tag Pattern Extractor allows bulk creation of master tag and alias tag patterns in the product database. This utility is an EXE file and is available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more
information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
You can generate XML load files (which contain multiple tag patterns and tag naming system items) by processing a Microsoft Excel file using this utility. These output XML load files can be loaded into the product database using the File >
Loader command in the Desktop Client.
The tags matching the tag patterns in the XML loader files are not extracted by the content discovery task if the INI file for these patterns is not updated. In order to update the INI file, update and save a tag discovery pattern in the
Data Capture Administration module. Now you can use the tag patterns in the XML loader files to run a content discovery task.
This application currently supports only English language in Microsoft Excel.
Generate multiple tag patterns
 You must have Microsoft Access Database Engine installed on your computer to use the Tag Pattern Extractor.
1. Open the TagPatternsExtractor.exe file downloaded from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
2. In the Tag Pattern Extractor dialog box, click Browse to upload the Microsoft Excel file containing the tag patterns and tag naming system items. The Microsoft Excel template file is from sample data located on Smart Community
<https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.
3. Click Generate. After generating the XML load files, the icons of the output files appear in the Tag Pattern Extractor dialog box.
 Two output files are generated and they are placed in the sample data folder. The sample data folder can be downloaded from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find
sample data on Smart Community. The output files include:
Tag Patterns - The XML load file for all the tag patterns.
Tag Naming System Items- The XML load file for the tag naming system items.
Viewing tag and document properties
The Tag module allows you to search for tags that are available in the database and view the tag properties.
The Documents module allows you to search for documents that are available in the database and view the document properties and relationships. You can view the thumbnails of the files and also open the actual files in their native
application. You cannot view the duplicate documents in the document explorer module.
 You must set the active scope before using the Data Capture Task Manager module. For more information, see Setting the active scope.
 You can customize the icons for the sub-modules in the Data Capture Task Manager module by adding the corresponding GIF files to the [drive]:\ Server Files\Web_Sites\[site_name]\Icons folder.
The name of the GIF files for the Data Capture Task Manager modules must follow this naming convention: Explorer_DisplayAs. For example, Explorer_Documents.gif for the Documents module.
The name of the GIF files for the Data Capture Task Manager sub-modules must follow this naming convention: Explorer_GroupName_DisplayAs. For example, Explorer_Tags_Mastertags for the Master Tags sub-module.
View master tag properties
Master tag patterns and alias tag patterns are defined in the Tag Discovery Pattern module. A master tag pattern can exist by itself or may be related to one or more alias tag patterns. A tag that matches the master tag pattern results in a
master tag. The master tag represents an actual object in an engineering plant.
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Task Manager.
3. Click Tags > Master Tags.
4. Search for the master tag that you want to view.
5. Select a value from the Column Set box, and then enter the name of the tag in the Search Master Tags box to view the properties of the master tag.
 To generate a document report, select a tag, and then click Generate Report 
. A tag document report includes the following: tag properties, documents related to the tag and list of alias tags related to the master tag.
View inconsistencies in master tag properties
You can view the inconsistencies in the properties and the property values of a selected tag available in different domains. If the property values of a tag do not match the corresponding values in the domain tag, the tag name and the
property values of the tag appear in red.
You can use the Generate Inconsistency Report 
 option to create a tag inconsistency report of the master tags in Microsoft Excel.
 You can tell the software to apply case insensitivity when checking for data inconsistencies, by selecting the option Enable case insensitivity when comparing data for inconsistencies in the Data Capture General Settings
module. For more information, see Learn more about defining the general settings.
1. On the Master Tags page, select a value in the Column Set Only box. The Column Set Only box lists the column sets configured in the Property Lists module. For more information on how to configure a column set, see Create a
column set.
 You must select a column set to generate an inconsistency report with complete information.
2. Search for the master tag that you want to view.
3. Select one or more tags from the search results, and then click View Inconsistency 
. The Tag Inconsistency dialog box displays the tag information for the first tag selected in the list.
View alias tag properties
A tag that matches the alias tag pattern results in an alias tag. An alias tag does not represent an actual object and is always related to a master tag. It interprets the master object that is represented by the master tag that it is related to.
1. Click Tags > Alias Tags.
2. Search for the alias tag that you want to view.
View documents
You can search for documents by name. The search results display the thumbnail of the master file attached to the documents. If a thumbnail does not exist for the master file, then an empty tile is displayed with the document name as the
header.
 You must search for any updated documents every time you open the document explorer module.
1. Click Documents.
2. In the Search Documents box, type the name of the document that you want to search for.
3. Click Search 
. The document relationships and all the configured document naming system items are displayed in the grid view.
4. Select one or more documents and then click Create Content Discovery Task 
 to create a content discovery task for the selected documents.
 You cannot create a content discovery task for duplicate documents.
In the Grid View, click the name of the file to view the file in the native application.
Using the thumbnail tile, you can do the following:
Click the document name in the header to open the file in the native application.
Double-click the header to maximize the thumbnail of the file.
To open selected items in SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client, select the objects in the item list and then click Open Items in SmartPlant Foundation Client 
.
You can click Export to Excel 
 to export the contents of the current view to a Microsoft Excel file.
To generate a document report, select a document, and then click Generate Report 
. For more information, see View an Open XML report in the Desktop Client.
Configuring Data Capture
 SmartPlant Foundation IM 2019 does not support Data Capture; you can use the Data Capture feature only with SDx. However, if you are a SmartPlant Fusion 2018R1 user, you can use the Data Capture feature with IM
2019, provided you have upgraded from SmartPlant Fusion 2018R1 to IM 2019 (any update).
This section contains information about configuring Data Capture.
Introduction to Data Capture Administration module in the Desktop Client
The Data Capture Administration module is delivered and installed with the product. This module enables you to manage the central settings, data reader patterns, document discovery patterns, document naming system, tag
classification, tag discovery patterns, property lists, and tag naming system.
 You must set the active scope before using the Data Capture Administration module.
Roles govern the user access to commands and data. Users perform different roles in different configurations (different plants or projects).
As a Data Capture administrator, you can configure new roles.
You can also create users and assign Data Capture related roles to the users.
Users and roles
The security model is a flexible mechanism to control role-based user access to data and the operations that can be performed on that data.
The security model is comprised of:
Users
Roles, domains, and access groups
Configurations
Role assignments and owning groups
Security rules (are used in the Web Client)
A user is assigned a role in a configuration for example, to work as a Designer in Project1. Roles are related to access groups, which control access to methods (commands), relationships, and security rules. Any number of methods or
relationships can be grouped together on an access group. A role can provide functionality through any number of access groups, and the same user can play different roles depending on the configuration.
Users and objects, like documents, can both be related to specific security codes to limit access, and security rules can be used to further restrict access and are related through the access group. For more information about these security
components, see Components of the security model.
The security model controls user access to:
Application modules
Menus and toolbars
Shortcut menu commands
Relationship manipulation and navigation
Data segregation based on user and tool ownership
Conditional data access supported by query
 The users and roles are configured by the administrator.
The following roles and their access groups are delivered with Data Capture:
Access group
Access group description
Roles
Data Capture Administrator
Data Capture Controller
Data Capture Reviewer
Data Capture Viewer
DataCaptureAdmin
Configure Data Capture for users
DataCaptureDocControl
Create and run the Document Discovery
Tasks and Content Discovery Tasks
DataCaptureDocUpdate
Update the documents
DataCaptureReviewer
Review the documents, tags and their
relationships
DataCaptureViewer
View documents, tags and run reports
Security Model
The security model is used to control role-based user access to the Data Capture modules.
Access groups govern the user access to commands and relationship creation and navigation. Roles are related to access groups. Access groups are related to methods, interfaces, and view definitions. This structure provides the roles
access to the different components of the system.
The following table displays the methods for the Data Capture modules.
Module name
Method name
Data Capture Administration module
DataCaptureAdministration Access
Data Capture Quality Control module
DataCaptureQualityControl Access
Data Capture Task Manager module
DataCaptureJobsAccess
Data Capture PreProcessor module
DataCapturePre-ProcessorModuleAccess
Access to the Data Capture modules are configured by relating the following access groups to the methods that are available with Data Capture.
DataCaptureAdmin access group is related to DataCaptureAdministrationAccess method, DataCaptureQualityControlAccess method, DataCaptureJobsAccess method, and DataCapturePreProcessorModuleAccess method.
DataCaptureReviewer access group is related to DataCaptureQualityControlAccess, and DataCaptureJobsAccess method.
DataCaptureDocControl access group is related to DataCaptureJobsAccess method and DataCapturePreProcessorModuleAccess method.
DataCaptureViewer access group is related to DataCaptureJobsAccess method.
The following table illustrates what access groups are related to which methods:
Access groups
Method name
FusionAdministration
Access
FusionQuality
ControlAccess
FusionJobs
Access
Fusion Pre-Processor ModuleAccess
DataCaptureAdmin
Yes
Yes
Yes
Yes
DataCaptureReviewer
No
Yes
Yes
No
DataCaptureDocControl
No
No
Yes
Yes
DataCaptureViewer
No
No
Yes
No
The access groups that are configured and delivered with Data Capture have access to the SmartPlant Foundation Client.
Methods are exposed to users through their relationships with roles by way of access groups -- access groups are associated with a role and their methods are then available to users in that role. For more information about setting up
security for your users, see Configuring security.
Set active scope
Before you create, modify, or view Data Capture objects, you must set your Desktop Client active scope to the Data Capture project.
1. Log on to the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > Set Active Scope to open the Set Active Scope dialog box.
 You can also set the active scope by clicking the text beside Selected Roles, Query Scope, or Create/Update Scope in the Desktop Client status bar.
3. In the Set Active Scope dialog box, set the scope to the required plant or configuration top, and click OK.
4. Select a plant or project from the Query Scope.
5. To set the create scope, select the plant or project from the Create Scope list.
 You can select only one plant or project for the create scope.
6. Click OK.
 In the Documents and Tags modules of Data Capture Task Manager, the query is performed based on the Query Scope configuration. For all the other modules, the query is performed based on the Create Scope
configuration without including higher configuration items.
Configure user access to Data Capture Task Manager sub-modules
As a Data Capture administrator, you can control user access to the Data Capture Task Manager sub-modules by configuring new methods.
1. Click File > New >Administration > Method in the Desktop Client.
2. Type a name and description for the new method in the Name and Description boxes respectively.
3. Select Explorer Configuration from the Client API list.
4. Type appropriate values in the Query Interface Name, ColumnSet to present results, and Group Name boxes. For example, if you want to configure Master Tag, type ISPFNMasterTag, FDWTagCS, and Tags in the respective
boxes.
5. Select the Available on Desktop client check box, and click Finish.
6. Right-click a method, and select Manage Access Groups to assign access groups.
Creating objects in Data Capture
As a Data Capture administrator, you can create a variety of objects within a project in the Desktop Client.
Create a master tag
1. Open the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > New > Tag.
3. In the New Tag dialog box, enter all mandatory information and click Finish. The master tag is created and appears in the New Items window.
Create an asset
1. Open the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Asset.
3. In the New Asset dialog box, select a type of asset and click Next.
4. Enter all mandatory information and click Finish. The asset is created and appears in the New Items window.
Create a manufacturer
1. Open the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Manufacturer.
3. In the New Manufacturer dialog box, enter the name in the Name box.
4. Type the required information, and click Finish.
Create a model
1. Open the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Model.
3. In the New Model dialog box, select a model type and click Next.
4. Enter all mandatory information and click Finish. The model is created and appears in the New Items window.
Create a supplier
1. Open the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > New > Model and Asset > Supplier.
3. In the New Supplier dialog box, enter all mandatory information and click Finish. The supplier is created and appears in the New Items window.
Create a system
1. Open the Desktop Client.
2. Click File > New > Organization/Support Items > System.
3. In the New System dialog box, enter all mandatory information and click Finish. The system is created and appears in the New Items window.
Configure Data Capture settings
In the Central Settings module, you can set various options to configure the Data Capture modules.
These options include:
Character Ranges - Allows you to add and delete the character ranges which can be addressed by Data Capture.
File Types - Allows you to add file types and relate them to Data Capture readers.
General Settings - Allows you to:
Set the file path to the location in which the ignored files must be stored.
Add the period of time for which the software waits for SmartPlant Markup.
Permit the software to generate thumbnails and resolve the duplicate files automatically.
 Data Capture will wait for a response from the SmartPlant Markup application for the period of time specified here, and will thereafter add a failure message to notify the users that SmartPlant Markup was unavailable.
Reader Settings - Allows you to relate each reader existing in the software with an application.
Resolve Duplicates Color Settings - Allows you to select colors to represent the file statuses in the Data Capture Resolve Duplicates module.
Resolve Relationships Color Settings - Allows you to select colors to represent the document relationship updates in the Data Capture Quality Control module.
Separators and Delimiters - Allows you to add separators that are used to separate file parts in the document discovery patterns and tag parts in the tag discovery patterns. It also allows you to add delimiters that will be used to
identify the end of a tag.
Vault UNC Path - Allows you to provide one or more UNC path to a shared directory that will contain the files that are to be loaded into database, and are accessible from many computers.
View Definition Groups – Allows you to create a view definition group with one or more view definitions. These view definition groups are used in the managed data tasks that use Data Validator for transferring data from database to
Facility Data Warehouse.
Character Ranges page
Allows you to add and delete the character ranges that will be supported by Data Capture.
Add Character Range - Allows you to set a character range.
Delete Character Ranges - Allows you to delete one or more character ranges.
Name - Type a name for the character range set.
Upper Case Range - Allows you to type the uppercase character range. For example, [A-Z].
Lower Case Range - Allows you to type the lowercase character range. For example, [a-z].
 The character range should be enclosed within square brackets and the characters should be separated by a hyphen.
Default - Allows you to set the selected character range set as default. The selected character range set is displayed by default in the Character Range list of the Document Discovery Pattern and Tag Discovery Pattern module.
File Types page
Allows you to add and delete file types from the list of those that are supported and also allows you to assign Data Capture readers for each file type.
Add New File Type - Allows you to add a file type to the list.
Configure Alternate Renditions - Allows you to associate an application for converting a file type to .pdf or generating a thumbnail.
Remove File Types - Allows you to delete one or more file types from the list.
Name - Select a file type from the list.
Description - Displays the description for the selected file type.
Reader - Select a Data Capture reader from the list to relate the selected reader to the file type.
Extension - Displays the file type extension for the selected file type.
Application - Select an application from the list to relate the selected application to the file type.
Select a file type and click the Configure Alternate Rendition option to set the .pdf and thumbnail rendition application.
PDF Rendition Options
Use Markup for PDF Rendition - Select this option to use SmartPlant Markup Plus for .pdf generation.
PDF Rendering Application - Type an alternate rendering application (other than SmartPlant Markup Plus) to use.
PDF Rendition Conversion Path - Type an alternate rendering conversion path.
Thumbnail Rendition Options
Use Markup for PDF Rendition - Select this option to use SmartPlant Markup Plus for thumbnail generation.
You can select an application or a reader for each file type.
You can select an application and a reader for a file type. If an application and reader is selected for a file type then the application take precedence. For example, if you select Image Reader (related to ABBYY application) as reader
and SmartPlant Markup as application for the PDF file type, then all the PDF files in the database get processed through the SmartPlant Markup application.
For PDF files and Microsoft Office files, by default PDF Reader is set as the base reader. The default application for PDF files is set as SPFNPDF. When extracting content from any file types other than PDF files, the PDF reader will
generate Markup renditions which are used by the software to retrieve the tag details.
General Settings page
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see General Settings page (modified in an update).
Allows you to set the file path for vaults and ignored files. In addition to this, you can allow automatic generation of thumbnails and set the wait time for SmartPlant Markup.
Ignored Files Path - Allows you to define the file path for storing ignored files.
 Click Browse 
, and select the folder to store the ignored files.
Markup Time Out - Allows you to define the period, in seconds, for which the application waits for a response from SmartPlant Markup. Default timeout is set to 500 seconds.
Auto Generate Thumbnails - Select this checkbox to allow the generation of thumbnail for each file during the document discovery task.
Auto Resolve Duplicates - Select this checkbox to allow the software to resolve the duplicate document conflicts. This option is selected by default.
Auto Generate PDF Files- Select this checkbox to allow the generation of a PDF for each file during the document discovery task.
Select the Use SmartConverter for content extraction from drawing files check box to extract tags from drawing files using the SmartConverter and SPFHotSpotter.ini file.
 To extract tags from drawing files using the SmartSketch, clear this check box and configure the SmartConverter with the following settings:
In My Computer > Properties, select the Allow check box for the following items to grant security permissions to the local service account:
Remote Launch
Remote Activation
For more information on how to configure the SmartConverter, see Configuring the SmartConverter for Graphical Navigation.
Enable case insensitivity when comparing data for inconsistencies - Select this checkbox to ignore case sensitivity, when checking for inconsistencies present in property values of a selected tag available in different domains.
 This option can be set when generating inconsistency results using the following options:
In the Data Capture Tags module > Master Tags page. For more information, see View inconsistencies in master tag properties.
In the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client, search for a Data Capture Inconsistency report and right-click Run Report to generate the report.
Mandatory values are marked by an asterisk (*).
When this Auto Resolve Duplicates check box is selected, if a new file is found in the document discovery task having modified date falling after the modified date of the master file or the candidate file attached to the document, the
software attaches the new file to the latest version of the document, instead of creating a duplicate document. The creation of new version of the document is described for the following cases:
Case I
Scenario: A document exists with a master file attached to it, and a new file is found in the document discovery task, having modified date falling before the modified date of the master file attached to the document.
Result: The new file is ignored and is not loaded into the database.
Case II
Scenario: A document exists with a master file and two candidate files attached to it, and a new file is found in the document discovery task, having modified date falling after the modified date of the master file attached to the
document.
Result: The new file gets attached to the latest version of the document as the master file and the candidate files are also moved to the latest version.
Case III
Scenario: A document exists with a master file and two candidate files attached to it, and a new file is found in the document discovery task, having modified date falling after the modified date of one of the candidate file attached to
the document.
Result: The new file gets attached to the latest version of the document as the candidate file. The master file, and the other candidate file are also moved to the latest version.
Reader Settings page
Allows you to relate an application for each reader.
Reader - Displays all the readers available in the application.
Application - Select an application from the list to relate the selected application to the reader.
Resolve Duplicates Color Status page
Defines the color settings for each file status that can be set on the duplicate files in the Data Capture Operations module.
Color Picker - Allows you to set the color for each file status.
Reset Colors - Reverts the colors for all the file statuses back to the default values.
Resolve Relationships Color Settings page
Defines the color settings for the updates made to the document relationships in the Data Capture Quality Control module.
Color Picker - Allows you to set the color for document relationship modification.
Reset Colors - Reverts the colors set for all the document relationship modifications back to the default values.
Separators and Delimiters page
The separators and delimiters that exist in the SDx Operations database are displayed on this page. Delimiters are internally used by the software for extraction of tags that have delimiters at the end of the tag.
Add Separators - Allows you to add one or more separators.
Delete Separators - Allows you to delete the selected separators.
Add Delimiters - Allows you to add one or more delimiters.
Delete Delimiters - Allows you to delete the selected delimiters.
Ignore Delimiters - Select this option if you want the software to ignore the delimiters while searching for tags in the content file.
UNC Path page
Allows you to add and delete UNC paths that contain the files that need to be processed in the document discovery task.
Add UNC path - Allows you to add a UNC path to the list.
Delete UNC paths - Allows you to delete one or more UNC paths from the list.
Name - Type a name for the UNC path.
UNC Path - Allows you to copy and paste a UNC path.
 You can also click Browse to navigate to the shared directory.
View Definitions Group page
Allows you to create view definition groups with one or more view definitions in each group.
Create View Definitions Group - Allows you to add a view definitions group to the list.
Edit View Definitions Group - Allows you to edit an existing view definitions group from the list.
Delete View Definitions Group - Allows you to delete one or more view definitions group from the list.
Name - Displays all the view definitions groups.
View Definitions - Displays the view definitions in each group.
Add languages and set the character ranges
The Character Ranges page allows you to add a range of characters from a character set. The characters within the defined character set are processed by the application during document discovery and content discovery.
View language and character ranges
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click Character Ranges to view the Character Ranges page, where the character ranges are displayed in an item list.
Add a language and the character ranges
1. In the Character Ranges page, click Add Character Range 
 to add a new row at the bottom of the list.
2. Type a name for the character range set in the Name column.
 You cannot change the name of the character range set once it is saved to the database.
3. Click the Upper Case Range column, and type the range of uppercase characters enclosed within square brackets and separated by a hyphen. For example, for English language type [A-B].
4. Click the Lower Case Range column, and type the range of lowercase characters enclosed within square brackets and separated by a hyphen. For example, for English language type [a-b].
5. Click the Default column to set the selected character range set as default.
Manage file types and prioritize them for content extraction
The File Types page allows you to add a file type to the list of supported file types. You can relate a file type to an existing Data Capture reader to indicate that the files with that extension can be processed by the specified Data Capture
reader. You can also relate a file type to an application to indicate that the files with that extension can be processed by the specified application.
You can select an application and a reader for a file type. If an application and reader is selected for a file type then the application take precedence. For example, if you select Image Reader (related to ABBYY application) as reader
and SmartPlant Markup as application for the PDF file type, then all the PDF files in the database get processed through the SmartPlant Markup application.
For PDF files and Microsoft Office files, by default PDF Reader is set as the base reader. The default application for PDF files is set as SPFNPDF. When extracting content from any file types other than PDF files, the PDF reader will
generate Markup renditions which are used by the software to retrieve the tag details.
You can also set the priority for processing the various file types listed in this page.
View the file type hierarchy
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click File Types to view the File Types page where the defined file types are displayed with their related reader.
Set the priority for the file types
If a document has files of different file types attached to the latest version, then the file with the extension that is at the top of this list will be set as master and will be further processed for the content and tag extraction.
1. On the File Types page, click the up arrow
 to move the selected file type to the top of the list.
2. Click the down arrow 
 to move the selected file type to the bottom of the list.
3. Click Save.
Add a file type hierarchy
1. On the File Types page, click Add file type 
 to add a new row to the bottom of the list.
2. Click 
 in the Name column, and select a file type from the list.
 The Description column and the Extension column are populated with corresponding values for the selected file type.
 You can add only file types that are supported by SmartPlant Foundation. The description and extension for the selected file type are populated from the information available in the database.
3. Click 
 in the Reader column, and select a reader from the list to relate it to the newly added file type.
4. Click 
 in the Application column, and select an application from the list to relate it to the newly added file type.
5. Click Save.
Delete a file type hierarchy
1. On the File Types page, select one or more file types from the item list.
2. Click Delete 
, and then click Yes to confirm the deletion.
Configure alternate renditions
1. In the File Types page, select one or more file types from the item list.
 If you select more than one file type, the PDF Rendition Conversion Path is unavailable.
2. Select Configure Alternate Renditions 
 to open the Configure Alternate Renditions window.
3. In the Configure Alternate Renditions window, you can configure PDF renditions in three ways. They are as follows:
Select the Use Markup for PDF Rendition check box.
When the Use Markup for PDF Rendition check box is selected a relationship is created between the selected, file type and the PDF file type.
A property named Alternate Rendition Application is set on this relationship indicating the application that is used to generate the PDF files. The value of this property is dmredl -p obj.Name -pdf $OUTPUTDIR.
Enter the appropriate value in the PDF Rendering Application box.
Enter the appropriate value in the PDF Rendition Conversion Path box.
4. You can configure the thumbnail rendition as follows:
Select the Use Markup for Thumbnail Rendition check box.
 When the Use Markup for Thumbnail Rendition check box is selected a relationship is created between the selected file type and the PNG file type.
5. Select Save.
Learn more about defining the general settings
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Learn more about defining the general settings (modified in an update).
The Define General Settings page allows you to add and update application-level settings.
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings to view the Define General Settings page. You can customize any of the following settings as per your requirement:
In the Ignored Files Path box, type a file path that will contain the ignored files.
Type a numeric value that represents the period of time, in seconds, up to which the application will wait for a response from SmartPlant Markup.
Select the Auto Generate Thumbnails check box to allow thumbnail generation for each file during the document discovery task.
 You can clear the Auto Generate Thumbnails check box to avoid the generation of thumbnails during the document discovery task. Thumbnail generation takes considerably more time to complete the document
discovery task.
Select the Auto Resolve Duplicates check box to allow the software to resolve the duplicate document conflicts.
 When this Auto Resolve Duplicates check box is selected, if a new file is found in the document discovery task having modified date falling after the modified date of the master file or the candidate file attached to the
document, the software attaches the new file to the latest version of the document, instead of creating a duplicate document.
Select the Auto Generate PDF check box to allow generation of .pdf files for each file during the document discovery task.
All .pdf files generated by the Document Discovery Task are in the PreProcessedAlternateRenditions folder. This folder is in the same location as the native files before the Document Discovery Task. All generated .pdf files
are available in the database.
You can provide .pdf files present in the PreProcessedAlternateRenditions folder as an input to the PDF reader preprocessor module for generating the corresponding content files.
Select the Use SmartConverter for content extraction from drawing files check box to extract tags from drawing files using the SmartConverter and SPFHotSpotter.ini file.
 To extract tags from drawing files using the SmartSketch, clear this check box and configure the SmartConverter with the following settings:
In My Computer > Properties, select the Allow check box for the following items to grant security permissions to the local service account:
Remote Launch
Remote Activation
For more information on how to configure the SmartConverter, see Configuring the SmartConverter for Graphical Navigation.
Select the Enable case insensitivity when comparing data for inconsistencies checkbox to ignore case sensitivity, when checking for inconsistencies present in property values of a selected tag available in different
domains.
 This option can be set when generating inconsistency results using the following options:
In the Data Capture Tags module > Master Tags page. For more information, see View inconsistencies in master tag properties.
In the SmartPlant Foundation Desktop Client, search for a Data Capture Inconsistency report, right-click on it and select Run Report to generate the report.
Click Save.
Manage reader and application relationship
The Reader Settings page allows you to relate each reader existing in the software with an application.
The following table illustrates what types of applications can be associated with a specific reader.
Reader
Application
3D Model Reader
SPFN3D, SPFNABBYY, SPFNAdlib
Document Reader
SmartPlantMarkup, SPFNABBYY, SPFNAdlib

SPFNABBYY, SPFNAdlib

Relate a reader to an application
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click Reader Settings to view a list of readers and their related applications.
5. Double click the fields in the Application column to enable 
.
6. Click 
 in the Application column, and select an application from the list to relate it to the reader.
Change the color settings for the file statuses
The Resolve Duplicates Color Settings page allows you to set a color to represent each file status in the Data Capture Operations module.
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click Resolve Duplicates Color Settings to view the Resolve Duplicates Color Settings page.
5. For each file status, click 
 on the color picker and select a color to represent that status.
6. Click Save.
Change the color settings for relationship changes
The Resolve Relationships Color Settings page allows you to set a color to represent each document relationship update status in the Data Capture Quality Control module.
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click Resolve Relationships Color Settings to view the Resolve Relationships Color Settings page.
5. For each document relationship update status, click 
 on the color picker and select a color to represent that document relationship updates.
6. Click Save.
Manage the separators and delimiters
The Separators and Delimiters page allows you to add and delete separators that can be used within file parts to create document discovery patterns and tag parts to create tag discovery patterns. You can also add and delete delimiters
that are used to identify the end of a tag.
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click Separators and Delimiters to view the list of separators and delimiters used in the application.
5. In the Separators section, type a separator in the Enter Separators box, and click Add Separators 
 to add a new separator.
6. Select one or more separators and click Delete Separators 
 to remove the selected separators from the list.
7. In the Delimiters section, type a delimiter in the Enter Delimiters box, and click Add Delimiters 
 to add a new delimiter.
 Select the Ignore Delimiters check box if you want the software to ignore the delimiters while searching for tags in the content file. For example, if you have the tag P-100 in the content file and you are using the tag
pattern P-### to search for tags, you may select the Ignore Delimiters check box for extracting P-100 from the content file.
8. Select one or more delimiters and click Delete Delimiters 
 to remove the selected delimiters from the list.
9. Click Save to save the changes.
The delimiters are internally used by the software for tag extraction. The software looks for the tags that end with the characters defined as delimiters. For example, if you have the pump numbers P-100, P-101, P-102 in a content file,
and you are using the tag pattern P-### with comma "," as delimiter to search for tags, then all the tags get extracted.
Separator and Delimiter consist of a single character. If you add more than one character in the Enter Separators box and click add, each character is added as a separator. For example, if you type {} and click add, then two
separators {and} get added.
Manage the UNC path
The UNC Path page allows you to add and update the UNC path where the files that are processed in the Document Discovery Task exist.
View a UNC path
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click UNC Path to view the UNC Path page, where the existing UNC paths are displayed in an item list.
Add a UNC path
1. In the UNC Path page, click Add UNC path 
 to add a new row at the bottom of the list.
2. Type a name for the UNC path in the Name column.
 You cannot change the name of the UNC path, after it is saved to the database.
3. Click the UNC Path column, and type the file path.
 You can click Browse to navigate to the folder that contains the files.
4. Click Save.
 Select the check box beside a UNC path to view it in the Choose File Directory page of the Document Discovery Patterns module.
Delete a UNC path
1. In the UNC Path page, select one or more UNC paths from the item list.
2. Click Delete UNC Paths
, and then click Yes to confirm the deletion.
Manage the view definition groups
The View Definitions Group page allows you to create and update the view definitions groups. These view definitions groups are used in the managed data tasks that use Data Validator for transferring data from database to the Facility
Data Warehouse.
 If you are trying to transfer business objects using Data Validator, you can transfer tags that correspond to specific documents directly without adding the tags to the work package. This can be done by creating a view definition
group with a document and tag pair that has same document interface. For more information, see Create a new work package and Create a new managed data task.
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Central Settings.
4. Click View Definitions Group to view the list of view definitions groups that already exist in the application.
5. On the View Definitions Group page, click Create View Definitions Group 
.
6. Type a name of the view definitions group in the Name box.
7. Type a keyword to search for view definitions in the Search View Definitions box.
8. Select one or more view definitions from the View Definitions pane, and click Select View Definitions 
.
9. Click OK to create a new view definitions group, and add it to the list.
Document Naming System (DNS)
The Document Naming System (DNS) enables you to define the parts of a document name, as well as to identify the names of the different parts of a document and relate them to existing objects in the database.
Data Capture is provided with the following properties that can be used to configure the document discovery patterns and set the properties on the files loaded into the database.
SPFNDiscipline
SPFNProject
SPFNUnit
SPFNDocType
SPFNClientDocTitle
SPFNClientDocRevision
SPFNDocArea
SPFNDocOriginator
SPFNDocSystem
SPFNDocParameter1
SPFNDocParameter2
SPFNDocParameter3
SPFNDocParameter4
SPFNDocParameter5
SPFNDocParameter6
SPFNDocParameter7
SPFNDocParameter8
SPFNDocParameter9
You can add additional document parameters in the Document Naming System page, by clicking on Add New Parameter 
 in the Attribute Name list and click Save.
The properties configured in the document discovery patterns are set on the files that are loaded into the database and can be viewed in the Properties window.
As the document controller, you can view the properties added by the administrator in the Desktop Client.
Using the Document Naming System module
The Document Naming System module is used to create, view, edit, and delete DNS items.
 Only administrators can access this module.
When you open the Document Naming System module, it displays the DNS items that already exist in the database in an item list.
View DNS items
1. Log in to SmartPlant Foundation.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Document Naming System to view the existing DNS items.
 By default, the Document Naming System page appears with the DNS items ParentDirectory and ParentParentDirectory. These DNS items can be used to define the folder and parent folder of the folder from which the files
are picked only as document attributes in Document Discovery Pattern module. These DNS items are not set for file parts.
Filtering the objects in the list view
1. To filter the results based on a specific value in any of the columns, click Filter 
 at the top of that column.
2. Select the property or properties to display the items based on the criteria in the item list.
 You can further refine the results by selecting a condition value, such as Is equal to or Starts with.
3. Click Filter to display the filtered results automatically in the item list.
 By filtering on several properties, you can refine the results to get to a specific set of data.
Create a DNS item
1. Click Create Document Naming System
 to add a new row in the bottom of the list.
2. Type a Name, Description, Display Name, Constant Value, Attribute Name, Document Relationship Name, and Related Interface Definition.
3. Type a value in the Constant Value field to define a fixed value for a DNS item.
4. To change the capitalization of the DNS item value, select an option from the Alter Case Options list.
You can select one of the following options:
None - Select this option if you do not want to change the capitalization of the value in the DNS item.
To Lower - Select this option if you want to change the value in the DNS item to lowercase.
To Upper - Select this option if you want to change the value in the DNS item to uppercase.
5. Type a Top Node Name and a Class Relationship Name. The DNS item appears under the defined Top Node Name only if you do either of the following:
In the Class Relationship Name column, type the name of the relationship that exists between the DNS item and the defined Top Node Name.
Type the Graphdef UID in the Class Relationship Name column. You can use a Graph Def to navigate to specific objects.
 The object name defined in the Top Node Name and derived from the DNS item should already exist in the database.
6. Click Save to add the new DNS item in the database.
 If you have accidentally deleted the DNS items ParentDirectory and ParentParentDirectory, then you have to create them with the same name to use as document attributes.
You can add additional document parameters in the Document Naming System page, by clicking on Add New Parameter 
 in the Attribute Name list and click Save. A display item and a column item is created for each newly
created document parameter.
Values for Name and Display Name are required.
The Name, Display Name, Attribute Name, and Document Relationship Name for DNS items must be unique values.
If the DNS item has a constant value defined, then the application does not validate the uniqueness of the Attribute Name and Relationship Name.
The Document Relationship Name must be in the format <Relationship_Name>_<Direction>. For example, SPFNDocumentUnit_12.
The display name of the document naming system is displayed as column header in the List view of the SmartPlant Desktop Client. If a document parameter is not selected to create a document naming system then it is displayed as
Attr <Number> in the List view of the SmartPlant Desktop Client.
Refresh the DNS item list
Click Refresh Document Naming System 
 to refresh the DNS item list with the latest data from the database.
 If you have unsaved data, it will be lost when the view is refreshed. A warning message appears to warn you that your data will be lost if you continue with the refresh operation.
Edit a DNS item
1. To edit a DNS item, double-click the row where it is listed.
2. Update the DNS item details, as necessary.
 The Name of a DNS item cannot be edited after it has been saved to the database.
3. Click Save to update the DNS item details in the database.
Delete a DNS item
1. Select one or more DNS items from the item list.
2. Click Delete 
, and then click Yes to confirm the deletion.
Document Discovery Pattern
The Document Discovery Patterns module allows you to define the file part names and map the part names to the standard nomenclature.
The document discovery pattern module allows the user to do the following tasks:
View the document discovery patterns available in the database.
Create new document discovery patterns.
Copy the existing file pattern to create new ones.
Modify the existing document discovery patterns.
Delete document discovery patterns.
Run a preview report.
View document discovery patterns
When you open the Document Discovery Patterns module, it displays the document discovery patterns that already exist in the database in an item list.
1. Log on to SmartPlant Dashboard.
2. Click Data Capture Administration to open the Data Capture Administration module.
3. Click Document Discovery Patterns to view the existing document discovery patterns.
You can click Preview Report to view a summary of the files in the folder that is mapped to the document discovery pattern. The report is displayed in a Microsoft Excel file.
You can view all the document discovery patterns including the ones at a higher configuration, but you cannot edit or delete those at a higher configuration.
Filtering the objects in the list view
1. To filter the results based on a specific value in any of the columns, click Filter 
 at the top of that column.
2. Select the property or properties to display the items based on the criteria in the item list.
 You can further refine the results by selecting a condition value, such as Is equal to or Starts with.
3. Click Filter to display the filtered results automatically in the item list.
 By filtering on several properties, you can refine the results to get to a specific set of data.
Create a new document discovery pattern
1. In the Document Discovery Patterns page, click Create Document Discovery Patterns 
.
 The Create Document Discovery Pattern page appears with the name of the UNC vault paths listed in the Choose File Directory list. If the client runs on the application server, the root directory names are also displayed.
 If the check box beside a shared path in the UNC Path page of the Central Settings module is selected, then the name of the selected UNC Path is displayed in the Choose File Directory list. The shared path is
displayed as a tooltip when you pause the cursor over the name of the shared path.
2. Click the Folder icon to open the folder and view the files and folders within it.
3. Click the folder name to select a folder.
 The Level to Start box contains the value 1 and the right pane appears with the folder selected in the left pane. The folder selected in the left pane is considered the first level.
4. In the right pane, click the Folder icon to open the folder and view the files and folders within it. Alternatively, you can also select a folder by specifying its path. Copy and paste the path of the folder, and click Next.
 The Number Of Levels box contains the folder level selected in the right pane and the level of the folder on the right pane is populated with respect to the Level to Start box value, which contains the folder level selected in
the left pane. By default, '*' is displayed in the Number of Levels box. If '*' is selected, all files are considered for extraction from all the sub-folders under the selected folder.
5. Type a specific file pattern in the Manual Pattern box. Alternatively, you can select Choose Files from File Directory option to select a file pattern from a list of unique file names.
 Select the Configure using Regular Expression check box, to create document discover patterns using regular expressions. For more information, see Edit the pattern for a file part.
6. Click OK to view the file pattern in the Document Definition Patterns page.
7. Select a file part tile, and then do the following to make changes to the file parts:
Add Part 
 - Allow you to add more file parts.
Split Part 
 - Allows you to split a file part into two or more file parts.
Merge Parts 
 - Allows you to combine two or more file parts into one file part.
Set Part 
 - Allows you to make changes to the file part. Select the File Type part, and click Set Part to select one or more file types.
8. Select a Document Naming System item from the drop-down list box to assign it to the file part.
 A warning message appears when a Document Naming System item is not assigned to one or more of the file parts.
9. Click Edit Document and Attribute Parts 
 to navigate to the Document Name Parts section.
10. Add or delete the file parts to set the document name pattern.
 You can add and delete constants in the document name pattern.
11. Click Document Attributes tab to navigate to the document attributes.
12. Add or delete document attributes to add additional attributes to the documents.
13. Click Save to save the document discovery pattern.
14. In the Create Document Discovery Pattern page, type a Name and Description for the document discovery pattern and click OK.
A warning message appears when the same Document Naming System item has been set for more than one file part.
If you want to divide a single word in the file name into two file parts, select None within the two file parts and assign a document naming system for each file part. For example, for a file name like 1409-DS10-144.dwg you can split
the part DS10 into two file parts with a None separator within them. The first part DS can be set to Document Type and 10 can be set to Unit.
When you save the document discovery pattern, the example file name pattern gets updated and saved. Alternatively, you can click Generate Example File Name 
 to update the example file name. The characters and symbols in
the example file name pattern are defined as per the following convention:
A is displayed for an Uppercase letter or *.
a is displayed for a Lowercase letter.
1 is displayed for a Number.
The values are displayed for the Constants.
Selected separators are displayed.
Manage file parts
Document discovery patterns consist of various file parts. You can perform various actions on the file parts to create the document discovery pattern.
Add a part
The Add Part 
 command allows you to add a new file part.
1. In the Document Discovery Parts page, select a file part.
2. Click Add Part 
.
3. Select an option to indicate where to add the new file part.
Add Left - Adds the new file part to the left of the selected file part.
Add Right - Adds the new file part to the right of the selected file part.
4. Click Accept Changes 
.
 If you want to abandon your changes, click Reject Changes 
.
5. Select a Document Naming System item from the drop-down list to assign it to the file name part.
If a Document Naming System item is defined as constant, then that item does not appear in the Document Naming System drop-down list of the file name part.
In the file parts list, Description is not a Document Naming System item. When you select Description as a file part from the list, the description value of the file attached to a document is displayed as the description value of
the document.
Delete a part
The Delete Part 
 command allows you to delete one or more selected file parts.
1. In the Document Discovery Parts page, select one or more file parts.
2. Click Delete 
.
Split a part
The Split Part 
 command allows you to split a file part into two or more file parts.
1. In the File Name Parts page, select a file part that you want to split.
2. Click Split Part 
 to split the selected part into two or more file parts.
3. In the Split Part section, click the splitter 
 between the two characters where you want to split the file part.
4. Click Accept Changes 
 to split the file part as indicated.
 If you want to abandon your changes, click Reject Changes 
.
 All the file parts resulting from the split are highlighted in blue.
Merge multiple parts
The Merge Parts 
 command allows you to merge two or more file parts into a single file part.
1. In the File Name Parts page, select two or more file parts that you want to merge.
2. Click Merge Parts 
 to merge the selected parts into a single file part.
Edit the pattern for a file part
The Set Part 
 command allows you to change the sequence of the characters that form a search pattern for each file part.
1. In the Document Discovery Parts page, select a file part for which you want to update the sequence of characters or the search pattern.
2. Click Set Part 
.
3. In the Options section, select Full String or Pattern from the Set By list. Depending upon the selection made here, the options change in the following pane.
Select the Full String option from the Set By list to represent each of the existing characters in the file part as a variable or constant.
Character Range - Select a character range set from the list to define characters from the selected set in the pattern. The character range set that is set as default in the Character Range page of the Central Settings
module is displayed by default in the list.
Variable - The characters in the string are set to variable. Additionally, the characters can be designated as an uppercase letter, lowercase letter, a number, or any combination of the three.
Constant - The characters in the string will be set to a constant value.
Select the Pattern option from the Set By list to build a specific pattern for the selected file part.
Character Range - Select a character range set from the list to define characters from the selected set in the pattern. The character range set that is set as default in the Character Range page of the Central Settings
module is displayed by default in the list.
 The uppercase, lowercase, and number options are available depending upon the character range set for the selected language in the Character Ranges page of the Central Settings module.
4. Click Accept Changes 
.
 If you want to abandon your changes, click Reject Changes 
.
 The characters set to pattern are displayed in a simple representation on the file part. When the cursor pauses over the file part, the specific pattern is displayed.
The pattern corresponding to each character in the simple representation is as follows:
A - Any uppercase letter
a - Any lowercase letter
# - Any digit from zero to nine
If you define a character to be either a letter or a number, then the character is displayed as a lowercase or uppercase letter depending upon the selection of lowercase or uppercase for alphabet.
The constant value in the file parts is displayed in bold.
New parts can use defined patterns, but only when the pattern is defined using the Set Part option.
Pattern Matching
